    Air Separation Unit 3
    NEneon×1
    LAliquid air×4 → NEneon×1
    Air Separation Unit 80s T6 110 kJ
    Draw the incondensable neon off the top of the main condenser of the air-separation column, where it concentrates.
    re_neon
    NGnitrogen gas×7 OGoxygen gas×2 AGargon gas×1
    LAliquid air×10 → NGnitrogen gas×7 OGoxygen gas×2 AGargon gas×1
    Air Separation Unit 90s T3 120 kJ +2 byproducts
    Rectify liquid air in a double column: counter-current distillation pulls volatile nitrogen (-196C) up and out the top, oxygen (-183C) collects at the bottom, and argon (-186C) is drawn from a side column. One feed, three pure products -- the cleanest multi-output in the plant.
    asu_rectify_air
    LAliquid air×10
    CAcompressed air×10 → LAliquid air×10
    Air Separation Unit 60s T3 220 kJ
    Compress filtered air, scrub out CO2 and moisture (they freeze and plug the column), then chill it against the cold product streams until it condenses. Liquefying the air is the energy-hungry step; the separation that follows is nearly free.
    asu_liquefy_air
    Aluminothermic Crucible 3
    FEferroniobium×1 SLslag×1
    NPniobium pentoxide (Nb2O5)×1 AIaluminum ingot×2 IOiron ore pellets×1 → FEferroniobium×1 SLslag×1
    Aluminothermic Crucible 100s T5 280 kJ +1 byproduct
    Aluminothermic reduction: ignite Nb2O5 with aluminium and iron oxide. The thermite-like reaction (3 Nb2O5 + 10 Al -> 6 Nb + 5 Al2O3) runs white-hot and yields ferroniobium directly, with alumina slag floating off -- exactly how FeNb is made for steel and superalloys.
    nb_aluminothermic
    NMniobium metal×1 SLslag×1
    NPniobium pentoxide (Nb2O5)×1 AIaluminum ingot×2 → NMniobium metal×1 SLslag×1
    Aluminothermic Crucible 100s T6 160 kJ +1 byproduct
    Aluminothermic reduction of white Nb2O5 with aluminium to pure niobium metal, alumina slag floating off. Unlike the ferroniobium route this skips the iron oxide, yielding nozzle-grade metal.
    re_niobium_metal
    CGcapacitor-grade tantalum powder×1 SLslag×1
    TPtantalum pentoxide (Ta2O5)×1 AIaluminum ingot×3 → CGcapacitor-grade tantalum powder×1 SLslag×1
    Aluminothermic Crucible 110s T5 310 kJ +1 byproduct
    Reduce Ta2O5 with aluminium to a high-purity tantalum metal powder. Its huge surface area and self-healing oxide are exactly what a capacitor anode needs.
    ta_reduce_powder
    Amine Gas Treater 1
    MGmethane gas×2 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1 HShydrogen sulfide gas×1
    RNraw natural gas×3 → MGmethane gas×2 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1 HShydrogen sulfide gas×1
    Amine Gas Treater 70s T4 90 kJ +2 byproducts
    Wash the raw gas with an amine solution to strip the acid gases: out comes sweet pipeline methane, plus the CO2 and hydrogen sulfide that each have to be dealt with separately.
    ng_amine_sweeten
    Anode Baking Furnace 1
    PCprebaked carbon anode×1
    CFcoke fuel×2 → PCprebaked carbon anode×1
    Anode Baking Furnace 75s T3 420 kJ
    Bind petroleum coke with pitch and bake it into a dense, conductive block. These prebaked anodes are fed into the pots and slowly eaten as the cell runs.
    al_bake_anode
    AOD Converter 1
    SSstainless steel ingot×2 SLslag×1
    SIsteel ingot×2 FEferrochrome×1 FEferronickel×1 BLburned lime (quicklime)×1 → SSstainless steel ingot×2 SLslag×1
    AOD Converter 140s T4 390 kJ +1 byproduct
    Argon-oxygen decarburization: blow an O2/Ar mix through a chromium-bearing melt. Diluting the oxygen with argon lets carbon burn out while chromium does NOT oxidise away -- so cheap high-carbon ferrochrome can be used. Makes ~75% of the worlds stainless steel.
    iron_aod_stainless
    APT Crystallizer 1
    APammonium paratungstate (APT)×2 SHsodium hydroxide×1
    STsodium tungstate solution×2 AGammonia gas×2 → APammonium paratungstate (APT)×2 SHsodium hydroxide×1
    APT Crystallizer 100s T4 120 kJ +1 byproduct
    Convert the tungstate solution to ammonium paratungstate and crystallise it out with ammonia. APT is the universal clean intermediate -- recrystallising rejects the last impurities, and some caustic is regenerated to the digester.
    w_crystallize_apt
    Assembly Cell 72
    TATVC actuator (EMA)×1
    SAsmall AC induction motor×1 TSturned steel shaft×1 ICinsulated copper wire×2 RSrolled steel plate×1 → TATVC actuator (EMA)×1
    Assembly Cell 90s T6 160 kJ
    Assemble an electromechanical actuator: motor, ballscrew and position-sensing coil.
    re_actuator_ema

    SSstainless steel sheet×6 AHautogenous heat exchanger×1 CIcryogenic insulation×2 LVlaboratory vacuum pump×1 PRpneumatic regulator×1 → ASAir Separation Unit×1

    AGacoustic glass-wool batt×2
    GFglass-fiber roving×2 → AGacoustic glass-wool batt×2
    Assembly Cell 45s T6 80 kJ
    Flame-attenuate and cross-lap the roving into a low-density glass-wool batt that traps sound.

    LIlithium-ion battery×1
    LIlithium-ion cell×3 DCdrawn copper wire×1 PRPVC resin×1 → LIlithium-ion battery×1
    Assembly Cell 70.0s 130.0 kJ
    time=70,energyamount=130,energyunit=kJ,categoryid=assembly • Station: assembler_workshop
    itemdb_battery_lithium_ion_assembler_workshop
    SZsilver-zinc battery×1
    SOsilver oxide×2 ZIzinc ingot×1 CPcaustic potash (KOH)×1 → SZsilver-zinc battery×1
    Assembly Cell 70s T6 120 kJ
    Silver(II)-oxide cathode + zinc anode in KOH -- a high-rate silver-zinc flight battery.
    re_battery_agzn
    ABacoustic blanket×2
    AGacoustic glass-wool batt×2 BCbeta-cloth cover×1 → ABacoustic blanket×2
    Assembly Cell 45s T6 80 kJ
    Quilt the glass-wool batt inside a sewn beta-cloth cover to make a fairing acoustic blanket.
    re_blanket_acoustic

    BCbeta-cloth cover×1
    GFglass-fiber roving×2 → BCbeta-cloth cover×1

    KGkerolox gas generator×1
    GGgas-generator chamber×1 GGgas-generator injector×1 STspark-torch igniter×1 TIturbine inlet manifold×1 TEturbine exhaust duct×1 PTpropellant tap valve×2 → KGkerolox gas generator×1

    ICinsulated copper wire×2 SNsignal NPN transistor×1 RSrolled steel plate×1 → STspark-torch igniter×1

    G/gimbal / TVC assembly×1
    SGspherical gimbal bearing×1 TATVC actuator (EMA)×2 → G/gimbal / TVC assembly×1

    Mount the engine on its spherical gimbal with a pair of actuators -- the complete steering joint.

    OGoptical gyroscope×1
    GCgyro cavity block×1 HRhigh-reflector cavity mirror×3 HNhelium-neon lasing gas×1 GDgas-discharge electrodes×1 FRfringe-readout photodetector×1 MDmechanical dither×1 → OGoptical gyroscope×1
    Assembly Cell 160s T6 240 kJ
    Optically contact three dielectric mirrors to a zero-expansion cavity block, fill it with helium-neon, strike the gas discharge and dither it so rotation reads as a beat frequency between the counter-rotating laser beams.
    re_gyro_rlg

    PCphotovoltaic cell×2 FCflight computer×1 ACattitude control array×1 AAaerospace aluminum alloy×2 → TDtechnology demonstrator satellite×1
    Assembly Cell
    Station: assembler_workshop
    itemdb_satellite_tech_demo_assembler_workshop
    SNseparation nut×2
    RSrolled steel plate×1 RERDX energetic charge×1 SIstandard initiator (NSI)×1 → SNseparation nut×2
    Assembly Cell 50s T6 90 kJ
    Build pyro nuts that release preloaded bolts without rebounding.
    re_separation_nut
    SSstage separation system×1
    FJfrangible joint×1 EBexplosive bolt×4 SNseparation nut×2 LSlinear shaped charge×1 → SSstage separation system×1
    Assembly Cell 110s T6 180 kJ
    Bundle the staging ordnance that parts one stage from the next.
    re_separation_system
    SCSiemens CVD Reactor×1
    SSstainless steel sheet×4 BGborosilicate glass tube×2 CAcarbon anode block×2 SUstep-up transformer×1 LVlaboratory vacuum pump×1 CBcopper bus bar×4 → SCSiemens CVD Reactor×1
    Assembly Cell
    Station: assembler workshop
    itemdb_siemens_cvd_reactor_assembler_workshop
    HShuge SPS solar array×1
    PCphotovoltaic cell×24 AAaerospace aluminum alloy×8 MPmicrowave power beamer×1 → HShuge SPS solar array×1
    Assembly Cell
    Station: assembler_workshop
    itemdb_sps_solar_array_huge_assembler_workshop
    SCstation core module×1
    AAaerospace aluminum alloy×4 BLbasic life-support loop×1 SDstandard docking port×2 FCflight computer×1 → SCstation core module×1
    Assembly Cell
    Station: assembler_workshop
    itemdb_station_module_core_assembler_workshop
    SSstation solar truss module×1
    PCphotovoltaic cell×8 AAaerospace aluminum alloy×4 RSrolled steel beam×2 SDstep-down transformer×1 → SSstation solar truss module×1
    Assembly Cell
    Station: assembler_workshop
    itemdb_station_module_solar_truss_assembler_workshop
    APaluminum propellant tank×1
    AAaerospace aluminum alloy×4 ASaluminum sheet×2 TAtitanium alloy plate×2 TPtank pressurization system×1 ASanti-slosh baffle×2 CIcryogenic insulation×2 → APaluminum propellant tank×1
    Assembly Cell
    Station: assembler_workshop
    itemdb_tank_propellant_aluminum_assembler_workshop
    FSfueled stage tank×1
    APaluminum propellant tank×1 DLdistilled LOX propellant×30 RKrefined kerosene propellant×13 → FSfueled stage tank×1
    Assembly Cell
    obtain=propellant_load • Station: assembler_workshop
    itemdb_tank_propellant_fueled_assembler_workshop
    RTregen thrust chamber×1
    MCmain combustion chamber×1 RNregen nozzle bell×1 NNniobium nozzle extension×1 STsintered tungsten rod×1 → RTregen thrust chamber×1
    Assembly Cell 140s T6 220 kJ
    SEPARATE PATH: stack chamber, tungsten throat, bell and niobium extension into a firing thrust chamber.
    re_thrust_chamber_assemble
    STstage thrust structure×1
    RSrolled steel beam×2 TAtitanium alloy plate×1 AAaerospace aluminum alloy×2 → STstage thrust structure×1
    Assembly Cell 120s T6 200 kJ
    Build the thrust box and aft skirt that carry engine thrust into the stage airframe.
    re_thrust_structure
    KTkerolox turbopump×1
    PIpump inducer×1 CIcentrifugal impeller×2 ITInconel turbine rotor×1 TSturned steel shaft×1 CBcryogenic ball bearing×2 LSlabyrinth seal×1 → KTkerolox turbopump×1
    Assembly Cell 130s T6 200 kJ
    SEPARATE PATH: assemble pre-made parts onto a common shaft into a complete turbopump.
    re_turbopump_assemble
    HPhigh-pressure turbopump×1
    KTkerolox turbopump×1 CIcentrifugal impeller×1 PIpump inducer×1 ISInconel superalloy ingot×2 → HPhigh-pressure turbopump×1
    Assembly Cell 180s T6 290 kJ
    Add a boost stage and a higher-pressure main impeller to the standard turbopump so it reaches the many-hundred-bar discharge a staged cycle needs.
    re_turbopump_staged_hp
    UCUranium Conversion Reactor×1
    SSstainless steel sheet×6 PPPVC pipe×4 PRpneumatic regulator×1 AHautogenous heat exchanger×1 → UCUranium Conversion Reactor×1
    Assembly Cell
    Station: assembler workshop
    itemdb_uranium_conversion_reactor_assembler_workshop
    VAVacuum Arc Remelt Furnace×1
    SSstainless steel sheet×4 LVlaboratory vacuum pump×1 SUstep-up transformer×1 CBcopper bus bar×4 AHautogenous heat exchanger×2 → VAVacuum Arc Remelt Furnace×1
    Assembly Cell
    Station: assembler workshop
    itemdb_vacuum_arc_remelt_furnace_assembler_workshop
    Basic Oxygen Furnace 1
    SIsteel ingot×3 SLslag×1
    PIpig iron ingot×3 SSsteel scrap×1 BLburned lime (quicklime)×1 → SIsteel ingot×3 SLslag×1
    Basic Oxygen Furnace 120s T3 335 kJ +1 byproduct
    Basic oxygen (Linz-Donawitz) process: blow pure O2 onto molten pig iron to oxidise out carbon, silicon, manganese and phosphorus (the heat is autogenous). ~25% scrap cools the blow; burnt lime fluxes P and S into a basic slag. The dominant primary steel route worldwide.
    iron_bof_steel
    Bayer Digester 1
    SAsodium aluminate liquor×3 RMred mud×2
    CBcrushed bauxite×3 SHsodium hydroxide×2 → SAsodium aluminate liquor×3 RMred mud×2
    Bayer Digester 120s T3 140 kJ +1 byproduct
    Cook the bauxite in hot, pressurised caustic soda. The alumina dissolves as sodium aluminate while iron oxide, silica and titania refuse to and settle out as red mud: ~1-1.5 t of red mud per t of alumina, the industry's great unsolved waste stream. Decant the clear liquor off the mud.
    al_digest_bauxite
    Beehive Coke Oven 2
    CFcoke fuel×1
    BCbituminous coal×1 → CFcoke fuel×1
    Beehive Coke Oven
    Station: coke_oven_beehive • Byproduct: coal_tar,gas
    itemdb_coke_fuel_coke_oven_beehive
    CFcoke fuel×10 CTcoal tar×1
    BCbituminous coal×12 → CFcoke fuel×10 CTcoal tar×1
    Beehive Coke Oven +1 byproduct
    Station: coke oven (beehive)
    itemdb_coal_tar_coke_oven_beehive
    Bessemer Converter 1
    SIsteel ingot×1
    PIpig iron ingot×1 BLburned lime (quicklime)×1 → SIsteel ingot×1
    Bessemer Converter
    Station: bessemer converter • Byproduct: refining_slag
    itemdb_steel_ingot_bessemer_converter_basic
    Blast Furnace 3
    CLcrude lead bullion×2 SLslag×1 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1
    LOlead oxide sinter×2 CFcoke fuel×1 LFlimestone flux×1 → CLcrude lead bullion×2 SLslag×1 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1
    Blast Furnace 220s T3 2155 kJ +2 byproducts
    Reduce the lead sinter with coke in a blast furnace to crude bullion. The silver in the ore reports almost entirely into the molten lead; iron and rock leave as slag.
    pb_blast_reduce
    PIpig iron ingot×2 SLslag×2
    IOiron ore sinter×3 CFcoke fuel×2 LFlimestone flux×1 → PIpig iron ingot×2 SLslag×2
    Blast Furnace 180s T2 1765 kJ +1 byproduct
    Charge sinter, coke and flux into the blast furnace. Coke burns to CO, which reduces the oxide (Fe2O3/Fe3O4 + CO -> Fe + CO2) as it descends; limestone fluxes the gangue to a fluid slag. Tap ~4%-carbon pig iron. A cleaner, more permeable burden than raw crushed ore.
    iron_blast_sinter
    PIpig iron ingot×1
    CHcrushed hematite×1 CFcoke fuel×1 LNlimestone nodule×1 → PIpig iron ingot×1
    Blast Furnace
    Station: small blast furnace • Byproduct: slag_ironmaking
    itemdb_pig_iron_ingot_blast_furnace_small
    Brine Treatment Unit 4
    PBpurified brine×2 TAtailings×1
    BSbrine solution×2 → PBpurified brine×2 TAtailings×1
    Brine Treatment Unit 45s T3 40 kJ +1 byproduct
    Pass the brine through ion-exchange softening to strip calcium and magnesium to ppb levels. Skip this and the cell membrane fouls within days -- brine purity is the hidden discipline of a chlor-alkali plant.
    ca_purify_brine
    BSbrine solution×2
    RHraw halite ore×2 RWraw water×1 → BSbrine solution×2
    Brine Treatment Unit 30s T2 20 kJ
    Dissolve rock salt in water to a saturated brine -- the raw feedstock for the whole plant. Salt is cheap and the sea is endless; the cost here is all electricity downstream.
    ca_dissolve_brine
    BSbrine solution×2
    RSrock salt×3 RWraw water×1 → BSbrine solution×2
    Brine Treatment Unit 40s T2
    Stir mined rock salt into water until no more will dissolve, giving a saturated brine. This is the hand way into the whole sodium and chlorine tree: it uses salt you can chip straight out of the ground, so the plant no longer waits on salt that only comes back as a by-product of titanium and zirconium work.
    salt_dissolve_brine_manual
    NCNPK compound fertilizer×3
    ANammonium nitrate×1 SSsingle superphosphate×1 MOmuriate of potash (KCl)×1 → NCNPK compound fertilizer×3
    Brine Treatment Unit 50s T5
    Blend the nitrogen source (ammonium nitrate), the phosphorus source (superphosphate), and the potassium source (muriate of potash) into one balanced NPK fertilizer.
    npk_blend
    Camp Marker 18
    ABadobe brick×4
    RCraw clay×2 LSloose sand×1 BObundle of thatch×1 RWraw water×1 → ABadobe brick×4
    Camp Marker 120s T1
    Mould clay, sand, and straw and dry the bricks in the sun, no kiln needed. The straw stops cracking. Weaker than fired brick and unhappy in steady rain.
    bld_form_adobe
    CMcement mortar×3
    PCPortland cement×1 LSloose sand×3 RWraw water×1 → CMcement mortar×3
    Camp Marker 40s T3
    Mix Portland cement with about three parts sand and water. It sets by hydration in hours and ends up much stronger than lime mortar.
    bld_mix_cement_mortar
    GCgreen clay brick×4
    RCraw clay×2 LSloose sand×1 RWraw water×1 → GCgreen clay brick×4
    Camp Marker 50s T1
    Press a stiff clay-and-sand mix into moulds and let it dry. The sand temper keeps the brick from cracking as it shrinks. Still needs firing to last.
    bld_mold_green_brick
    CBcob block×3
    RCraw clay×2 LSloose sand×2 BObundle of thatch×1 RWraw water×1 → CBcob block×3
    Camp Marker 90s T1
    Knead clay, sand, and straw into a stiff earth and build it up by hand. Slow, but the thick walls hold heat and cost almost nothing.
    bld_form_cob
    CCcured concrete×2
    WCwet concrete×2 → CCcured concrete×2
    Camp Marker 600s T3
    Leave fresh concrete damp to cure. Strength comes from a slow chemical set, not from drying, and keeps building for weeks. The long wait is the price of its strength.
    bld_cure_concrete
    WCwet concrete×4
    PCPortland cement×1 LSloose sand×2 CGcrushed gravel×3 RWraw water×1 → WCwet concrete×4
    Camp Marker 60s T3
    Mix cement, sand, and gravel to the one-two-three rule with water. Workable only briefly: the water has started the set, so place it before it stiffens.
    bld_mix_concrete
    BDblue dye×2
    BFblue flower petals×3 RWraw water×1 SLslaked lime×1 → BDblue dye×2
    Camp Marker 70s T1 90 kJ
    Mash scarce gentian petals and simmer them with a lime mordant into a precious blue dye.
    dye_boil_blue
    BDbrown dye×2
    CBcurled bark strip×2 RWraw water×1 → BDbrown dye×2
    Camp Marker 55s T1 80 kJ
    Boil tannin-rich bark in water to draw out a cheap, colourfast brown dye.
    dye_boil_brown
    PDpurple dye×2
    PFpurple flower petals×3 RWraw water×1 SLslaked lime×1 → PDpurple dye×2
    Camp Marker 60s T1 90 kJ
    Mash violet and lavender petals and simmer them with a lime mordant into a soft purple dye.
    dye_boil_purple
    RDred dye×2
    RFred flower petals×3 RWraw water×1 SLslaked lime×1 → RDred dye×2
    Camp Marker 60s T1 90 kJ
    Mash red poppy petals and simmer them in water with a lime mordant into a red dye.
    dye_boil_red
    YDyellow dye×2
    YFyellow flower petals×3 RWraw water×1 SLslaked lime×1 → YDyellow dye×2
    Camp Marker 60s T1 90 kJ
    Mash yellow petals and simmer them in water with a pinch of slaked lime to fix the colour into a yellow dye.
    dye_boil_yellow
    LMlime mortar×3
    SLslaked lime×1 LSloose sand×3 RWraw water×1 → LMlime mortar×3
    Camp Marker 45s T1
    Cut slaked lime with about three parts sand and water. It sets slowly by taking carbon dioxide from the air, Ca(OH)2 + CO2 back to limestone, so it stays workable a long time.
    bld_mix_lime_mortar
    RErammed earth block×3
    LSloose sand×2 RCraw clay×1 CGcrushed gravel×1 RWraw water×1 → RErammed earth block×3
    Camp Marker 80s T2
    Tamp damp, gravelly earth hard inside a form until it sets like soft stone. Very strong under load, as long as it is kept dry.
    bld_ram_earth
    RCreinforced concrete×3
    WCwet concrete×3 SRsteel rebar×1 → RCreinforced concrete×3
    Camp Marker 600s T4
    Lay rebar in the form and pour concrete around it. Concrete takes the squeeze, steel takes the pull, and the long cure locks them together.
    bld_cast_reinforced
    TRthatched roof×1
    BObundle of thatch×6 RBrough board plank×2 → TRthatched roof×1
    Camp Marker 70s T1
    Lay bundled straw thick over light battens. Cheap and warm, but it rots and burns.
    bld_build_roof_thatch
    TRthatched roof×1
    BObundle of thatch×8 ROrope×4 → TRthatched roof×1
    Camp Marker 55s T0
    Lash bundles of dried thatch in overlapping rows to a stick frame with rope, working bottom to top so each row sheds rain over the one below. Keeps the weather off your bedroll and the wet off your fire. It eats a lot of cordage, so make rope first.
    roof_thatch_build_manual
    SLslaked lime×2
    BLburned lime (quicklime)×1 RWraw water×1 → SLslaked lime×2
    Camp Marker 30s T1
    Quench quicklime with water: CaO + H2O gives Ca(OH)2 and real heat, so it hisses and falls to powder. The binder for mortar and wash.
    bld_slake_lime
    HShard soap×3 CGcrude glycerin×1
    LYlye×1 G(grease (tallow)×2 SLsalt lick×1 → HShard soap×3 CGcrude glycerin×1
    Camp Marker 120s T1 130 kJ +1 byproduct
    Boil lye with rendered tallow until it saponifies, then salt it out into a hard bar soap; crude glycerin separates off as a by-product.
    soap_boil_hard
    Carbonation Reactor 18
    MEmethylenedianiline×1
    ANaniline×2 FOformaldehyde×1 → MEmethylenedianiline×1
    Carbonation Reactor 90s T6 140 kJ
    Condense two anilines with formaldehyde into methylenedianiline (MDA).
    re_mda
    RERDX energetic charge×2
    NAnitric acid×2 SAsulfuric acid×1 CGcrude glycerin×1 → RERDX energetic charge×2
    Carbonation Reactor 100s T6 180 kJ
    Nitrate with mixed acid into a cast RDX/HMX-class high explosive -- the shared energetic for pyro devices and gunpowder.
    re_energetic_rdx
    FOformaldehyde×1
    MEmethanol×1 OGoxygen gas×1 → FOformaldehyde×1
    Carbonation Reactor 60s T6 100 kJ
    Oxidise methanol over a silver catalyst to formaldehyde.
    re_formaldehyde
    GLglycerol×1
    RTrendered tallow×2 SHsodium hydroxide×1 → GLglycerol×1
    Carbonation Reactor 70s T6 90 kJ
    Boil tallow with caustic soda (saponification); the sweet liquor decanted off the soap is glycerol.
    re_glycerol
    TTTEA-TEB charge×2
    TRtriethylaluminum×3 TRtriethylborane×1 → TTTEA-TEB charge×2
    Carbonation Reactor 70s T6 140 kJ
    Blend triethylaluminum with about 15 percent triethylborane under a nitrogen blanket to make the pyrophoric TEA-TEB starter fluid.
    re_tea_teb_compound
    CIcryogenic insulation×3
    RPrigid polyurethane foam×2 ACagglomerated cork×1 → CIcryogenic insulation×3
    Carbonation Reactor 50s T6 90 kJ
    Spray rigid polyurethane foam and back it with agglomerated cork to insulate the cryogenic tank wall (SOFI + cork TPS).
    re_insulation_foam
    PMpolymeric MDI×1
    MEmethylenedianiline×1 PHphosgene×2 → PMpolymeric MDI×1
    Carbonation Reactor 110s T6 170 kJ
    Phosgenate MDA's amine groups into the isocyanate groups of polymeric MDI.
    re_pmdi
    LClithium carbonate (battery grade)×1
    LHlithium hydroxide monohydrate×2 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1 → LClithium carbonate (battery grade)×1
    Carbonation Reactor 90s T4 70 kJ
    Bubble CO2 through lithium hydroxide to drop battery-grade lithium carbonate: 2 LiOH + CO2 -> Li2CO3 + H2O. The classic lithium-ion feedstock.
    li_carbonate
    DTdinitrogen tetroxide×1
    NAnitric acid×2 → DTdinitrogen tetroxide×1
    Carbonation Reactor 80s T6 140 kJ
    Concentrate nitrogen oxides from nitric acid into storable dinitrogen tetroxide.
    re_n2o4
    NInitrobenzene×1
    BEbenzene×1 NAnitric acid×1 → NInitrobenzene×1
    Carbonation Reactor 70s T6 110 kJ
    Nitrate benzene with nitric acid to nitrobenzene.
    re_nitrobenzene
    PHphosgene×1
    CMcarbon monoxide×1 CGchlorine gas×1 → PHphosgene×1
    Carbonation Reactor 70s T6 120 kJ
    Combine carbon monoxide with chlorine over carbon to make phosgene (sealed reactor).
    re_phosgene
    PPpolyether polyol×1
    POpropylene oxide×3 GLglycerol×1 → PPpolyether polyol×1
    Carbonation Reactor 110s T6 160 kJ
    Grow propylene-oxide chains out from a glycerol initiator into a hydroxyl-terminated polyether polyol.
    re_polyether_polyol
    RPrigid polyurethane foam×2
    PPpolyether polyol×1 PMpolymeric MDI×1 RWraw water×1 → RPrigid polyurethane foam×2
    Carbonation Reactor 80s T6 120 kJ
    React polyol with polymeric MDI; the water blows CO2 to cure a rigid closed-cell SOFI foam.
    re_pu_foam_rigid
    POpropylene oxide×2
    PRpropylene×1 OGoxygen gas×1 → POpropylene oxide×2
    Carbonation Reactor 80s T6 130 kJ
    Epoxidise propylene with oxygen to the reactive epoxide propylene oxide.
    re_propylene_oxide
    ACammonium chloride×2
    AHammonium hydroxide×1 HAhydrochloric acid gas×1 → ACammonium chloride×2
    Carbonation Reactor 60s T6 90 kJ
    Neutralise ammonia liquor with hydrochloric acid to crystallise ammonium chloride.
    re_ammonium_chloride
    TDtitanium dioxide×1
    TTtitanium tetrachloride×1 OGoxygen gas×1 → TDtitanium dioxide×1
    Carbonation Reactor 60s T6 110 kJ
    Burn titanium tetrachloride in oxygen to white titanium dioxide (chlorine recycled back to the chlorinator).
    re_titania
    TRtriethylaluminum×2
    AIaluminum ingot×2 ETethylene×6 HGhydrogen gas×3 → TRtriethylaluminum×2
    Carbonation Reactor 120s T6 190 kJ
    Ziegler direct process: add hydrogen and ethylene across aluminum metal to build triethylaluminum.
    re_triethylaluminum
    TRtriethylborane×1
    BTboron trifluoride×1 TRtriethylaluminum×1 → TRtriethylborane×1
    Carbonation Reactor 100s T6 160 kJ
    Transmetalate: triethylaluminum hands its ethyl groups to boron trifluoride, yielding triethylborane.
    re_triethylborane
    Carving Bench 8
    TBtimber beam×1
    RTrough timber beam×2 WPwooden peg×2 → TBtimber beam×1
    Carving Bench 60s T1
    Peg a timber span to carry floors and roofs across an opening to the posts at each end.
    bld_build_beam_timber
    TFtimber floor×1
    RTrough timber beam×2 RBrough board plank×6 WPwooden peg×4 → TFtimber floor×1
    Carving Bench 80s T1
    Deck timber joists with planks to span a short gap. Light, but it droops under heavy load.
    bld_build_floor_timber
    OSobsidian scalpel×1
    PFpressure-flaked obsidian blade×1 CWconstruction wood×1 → OSobsidian scalpel×1
    Carving Bench 90s T3 30 kJ
    Haft an obsidian micro-blade to a carved wooden handle: a surgical-grade scalpel whose edge cuts between cells for clean, low-scarring incisions.
    ob_make_scalpel
    TPtimber post×1
    RTrough timber beam×2 WPwooden peg×2 → TPtimber post×1
    Carving Bench 60s T1
    Peg a squared post that carries weight straight down. Cheap and quick, but weak against sideways push.
    bld_build_pillar_timber
    TRtimber ramp×1
    RBrough board plank×6 RTrough timber beam×1 WPwooden peg×4 → TRtimber ramp×1
    Carving Bench 70s T1
    Plank a timber ramp to move carts, animals, and yourself up a level without steps.
    bld_build_ramp_timber
    TRtimber roof×1
    RTrough timber beam×2 RBrough board plank×6 → TRtimber roof×1
    Carving Bench 80s T1
    Plank a timber roof over a beam frame: solid, weather-tight cover, still flammable.
    bld_build_roof_timber
    TStimber stair×1
    RBrough board plank×6 RTrough timber beam×1 WPwooden peg×4 → TStimber stair×1
    Carving Bench 70s T1
    Peg a light timber stair between floors, easy to build and to replace.
    bld_build_stair_timber
    TFtimber-frame wall×1
    RTrough timber beam×2 RBrough board plank×4 WPwooden peg×4 → TFtimber-frame wall×1
    Carving Bench 80s T1
    Peg a timber frame and sheathe it in planks. Light and fast, but it carries less and it burns.
    bld_build_wall_timber
    Caustic Digester Autoclave 3
    LHlithium hydroxide monohydrate×2 SSsodium sulfate×1
    LSlithium sulfate liquor×2 SHsodium hydroxide×2 → LHlithium hydroxide monohydrate×2 SSsodium sulfate×1
    Caustic Digester Autoclave 110s T4 80 kJ +1 byproduct
    Causticise the liquor with caustic soda: Li2SO4 + 2 NaOH -> 2 LiOH + Na2SO4. The lithium hydroxide crystallises out and the sodium sulfate byproduct is recovered as a salable salt.
    li_causticize
    MHmagnesium hydroxide×1 TAtailings×1
    BSbrine solution×2 BLburned lime (quicklime)×1 → MHmagnesium hydroxide×1 TAtailings×1
    Caustic Digester Autoclave 80s T3 60 kJ +1 byproduct
    Stir lime into magnesium-bearing brine; magnesium drops out as a milky Mg(OH)2 precipitate, the same trick used to win magnesium from seawater.
    mg_precipitate_hydroxide
    STsodium tungstate solution×2 SLslag×1
    RWraw wolframite ore×2 SHsodium hydroxide×3 → STsodium tungstate solution×2 SLslag×1
    Caustic Digester Autoclave 110s T4 150 kJ +1 byproduct
    Boil wolframite in hot caustic soda under pressure: (Fe,Mn)WO4 + 2 NaOH -> Na2WO4 + Fe/Mn hydroxide slag. Tungsten dissolves as sodium tungstate, leaving the iron and manganese behind -- the first and biggest purity gain.
    w_digest_wolframite
    Caustic Evaporator 1
    SHsodium hydroxide×1 RWraw water×1
    DCdilute cell caustic×2 → SHsodium hydroxide×1 RWraw water×1
    Caustic Evaporator 60s T3 110 kJ +1 byproduct
    Boil the weak cell liquor down to ~50% caustic soda, recovering the water. Evaporation is energy-hungry in its own right, which is why caustic is sold by the tonne, not the litre.
    ca_concentrate_caustic
    Cement Ball Mill 1
    PCPortland cement×2
    CCcement clinker×2 RGraw gypsum ore×1 → PCPortland cement×2
    Cement Ball Mill 90s T4 90 kJ
    Grind clinker with a few percent raw gypsum as a set-retarder: Portland cement. Without that gypsum the cement would flash-set in the mixer before you could pour it.
    gyp_grind_portland
    Claus Sulfur Unit 1
    RSrecovered sulfur×3 RWraw water×2
    HShydrogen sulfide gas×3 OGoxygen gas×1 → RSrecovered sulfur×3 RWraw water×2
    Claus Sulfur Unit 80s T4 110 kJ +1 byproduct
    Run the toxic H2S through a Claus unit: 2 H2S + O2 -> 2 S + 2 H2O. A hazard becomes a product -- this is where most of the world's sulfur actually comes from.
    ng_claus_sulfur
    Clay Casting Pit 5
    ACAluminothermic Crucible×1
    SCsmall clay crucible×2 SRsilica refractory brick×4 HIhammered iron plate×2 → ACAluminothermic Crucible×1
    Clay Casting Pit
    Station: clay casting pit
    itemdb_aluminothermic_crucible_clay_casting_pit
    CBconcrete block (CMU)×3
    WCwet concrete×2 → CBconcrete block (CMU)×3
    Clay Casting Pit 180s T3
    Cast fresh concrete in a hollow mould and cure it into the grey building block: fast, consistent, and strong.
    bld_cast_cmu
    CAcopper anode×2
    BCblister copper×2 → CAcopper anode×2
    Clay Casting Pit 60s T2 45 kJ
    Cast blister/fire-refined copper into flat anodes for the electrorefining tankhouse.
    cu_cast_anode
    GBgypsum board (drywall)×2
    POplaster of Paris×2 RWraw water×1 → GBgypsum board (drywall)×2
    Clay Casting Pit 40s T2 20 kJ
    Slurry plaster with water between paper sheets and let it re-hydrate to set rock-hard: gypsum board. The reaction is just the calcination run backwards.
    gyp_cast_board
    CMcast magnesium part×1
    MImagnesium ingot×2 AGargon gas×1 → CMcast magnesium part×1
    Clay Casting Pit
    Station: clay_casting_pit
    itemdb_magnesium_casting_clay_casting_pit
    Contact Converter (V2O5 beds, DCDA) 10
    ACacrylonitrile×1 RWraw water×3
    PRpropylene×1 AGammonia gas×1 OGoxygen gas×2 → ACacrylonitrile×1 RWraw water×3
    Contact Converter (V2O5 beds, DCDA) 100s T5 +1 byproduct
    The Sohio process: react propylene, ammonia and air in one pass over a bismuth-molybdate catalyst to make acrylonitrile and water (with a little HCN and acetonitrile as real-world by-products).
    acrylonitrile_ammoxidation
    CUcumene×1
    BEbenzene×1 PRpropylene×1 → CUcumene×1
    Contact Converter (V2O5 beds, DCDA) 70s T5
    Alkylate benzene with propylene over an acid catalyst to make cumene (isopropylbenzene).
    cumene_alkylate
    URurea×1 RWraw water×1
    AGammonia gas×2 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1 → URurea×1 RWraw water×1
    Contact Converter (V2O5 beds, DCDA) 70s T5 +1 byproduct
    Combine ammonia with carbon dioxide under pressure to make urea and water (2 ammonia + 1 carbon dioxide makes 1 urea + 1 water).
    urea_synthesis
    NOnitric oxide×4 RWraw water×6 PGplatinum gauze catalyst×1
    AGammonia gas×4 OGoxygen gas×5 PGplatinum gauze catalyst×1 → NOnitric oxide×4 RWraw water×6 PGplatinum gauze catalyst×1
    Contact Converter (V2O5 beds, DCDA) 60s T5 +2 byproducts
    Burn ammonia in air over the platinum screen to make nitric oxide and water (4 ammonia + 5 oxygen makes 4 nitric oxide + 6 water). The screen is required but passes through untouched.
    ostwald_ammonia_oxidation
    NDnitrogen dioxide×2
    NOnitric oxide×2 OGoxygen gas×1 → NDnitrogen dioxide×2
    Contact Converter (V2O5 beds, DCDA) 30s T5
    Let nitric oxide meet more air to form brown nitrogen dioxide (2 nitric oxide + 1 oxygen makes 2 nitrogen dioxide).
    ostwald_no_to_no2
    STsulfur trioxide gas×2
    SDsulfur dioxide gas×2 → STsulfur trioxide gas×2
    Contact Converter (V2O5 beds, DCDA) 30s T4 60 kJ
    Catalytic oxidation over vanadium(V) oxide beds: 2 SO2 + O2 <=> 2 SO3 (dH=-196 kJ/mol), ~450C and 1-2 atm. Exothermic + reversible, so heat is pulled between beds; double-contact/double-absorption (DCDA) pushes ~98%+ conversion and keeps SO2 stack emissions low.
    acid_so2_to_so3
    VCvinyl chloride monomer×2 HAhydrochloric acid gas×2
    EDethylene dichloride×2 → VCvinyl chloride monomer×2 HAhydrochloric acid gas×2
    Contact Converter (V2O5 beds, DCDA) 60s T5 +1 byproduct
    Crack ethylene dichloride to vinyl chloride, releasing hydrogen chloride that is recycled back into chlorine chemistry.
    vcm_crack
    PHphenol×1 ACacetone×1
    CUcumene×1 OGoxygen gas×1 → PHphenol×1 ACacetone×1
    Contact Converter (V2O5 beds, DCDA) 90s T5 +1 byproduct
    The cumene process: oxidise cumene in air to its hydroperoxide, then cleave it with acid. One pass yields phenol and acetone together -- conveniently the two halves of bisphenol-A.
    cumene_process_phenol
    PRpolyethylene resin×2
    ETethylene×4 → PRpolyethylene resin×2
    Contact Converter (V2O5 beds, DCDA) 80s T5
    Link ethylene units into polyethylene resin.
    pe_polymerize
    PRpolypropylene resin×2
    PRpropylene×4 → PRpolypropylene resin×2
    Contact Converter (V2O5 beds, DCDA) 80s T5
    Link propylene units into polypropylene resin over a Ziegler-Natta catalyst.
    pp_polymerize
    Copper Converter (Peirce-Smith) 2
    BCblister copper×1 SDsulfur dioxide gas×1 SLslag×1
    CMcopper matte×2 RSraw silica ore×1 → BCblister copper×1 SDsulfur dioxide gas×1 SLslag×1
    Copper Converter (Peirce-Smith) 200s T2 560 kJ +2 byproducts
    Peirce-Smith two-blow. Slag blow: 2FeS + 3O2 -> 2FeO + 2SO2, then 2FeO + SiO2 -> fayalite slag. Copper blow: Cu2S + O2 -> 2Cu + SO2. Taps blister copper, ~98-99.5% Cu.
    cu_convert_blister
    NCnickel converter matte (Ni3S2)×1 SLslag×1 SDsulfur dioxide gas×1
    NFnickel furnace matte×2 OGoxygen gas×1 → NCnickel converter matte (Ni3S2)×1 SLslag×1 SDsulfur dioxide gas×1
    Copper Converter (Peirce-Smith) 100s T3 280 kJ +2 byproducts
    Blow air/oxygen through the molten matte in a Peirce-Smith converter to oxidise out the remaining iron (slagged with silica), leaving high-grade Ni3S2 converter matte and more SO2 for the acid plant.
    nis_converter
    Cryogenic Helium Unit 1
    CHcrude helium×1 NGnitrogen gas×2
    MGmethane gas×4 → CHcrude helium×1 NGnitrogen gas×2
    Cryogenic Helium Unit 120s T5 200 kJ +1 byproduct
    Chill the sweet gas until everything but helium liquefies (nitrogen rejection). You process a huge volume of gas to skim off a little crude helium -- but natural gas is the only place on Earth helium is concentrated enough to catch.
    ng_cryo_recover_helium
    Cupellation Hearth 1
    SDsilver dore×1 L(litharge (PbO)×1
    SZsilver-zinc crust×1 OGoxygen gas×1 → SDsilver dore×1 L(litharge (PbO)×1
    Cupellation Hearth 150s T3 630 kJ +1 byproduct
    Cupel the crust under an air blast: zinc and lead oxidise to litharge and are absorbed/skimmed, leaving a noble button of dore silver. The oldest assay there is.
    pb_cupellation
    Czochralski Crystal Puller 1
    CSCzochralski silicon ingot×1
    EGelectronic-grade polysilicon×4 → CSCzochralski silicon ingot×1
    Czochralski Crystal Puller 180s T4 300 kJ
    Melt the polysilicon and pull a single crystal: dip a seed into the melt and draw it slowly upward while rotating, so one continuous, flawless lattice grows from the bath. The whole ingot is one crystal.
    si_czochralski_ingot
    Disc Pelletizer 2
    ICindurated chromite pellet×3
    CCcrushed chromite ore×3 → ICindurated chromite pellet×3
    Disc Pelletizer 45s T3 40 kJ
    Roll the chromite fines into green balls on the disc and fire them hard in the induration kiln. Indurated pellets keep the furnace burden permeable so reducing gas can climb through it.
    fcr_pelletize_chromite
    IOiron ore pellets×3
    IOiron ore concentrate×3 RCraw clay×1 → IOiron ore pellets×3
    Disc Pelletizer 80s T3 60 kJ
    Roll moist concentrate with ~1% bentonite binder (clay) into green balls on a disc, then indurate (fire) them hard. Uniform, high-Fe, high-strength pellets -- the ideal direct-reduction feed.
    iron_pelletize
    DRI Shaft Furnace 1
    SIsponge iron (DRI)×2 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1
    IOiron ore pellets×3 CFcoke fuel×2 → SIsponge iron (DRI)×2 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1
    DRI Shaft Furnace 160s T3 1120 kJ +1 byproduct
    Solid-state reduction: pellets meet coal/CO in a shaft or rotary kiln below irons melting point. Oxygen leaves as CO2 while the metal never liquefies, yielding porous metallic sponge iron (DRI). The low-energy, scrap-substitute charge for electric-arc steel.
    iron_dri_coal
    Drying Rack 4
    CFcured fox fur×1
    FPfox pelt×1 CBcurled bark strip×2 RWraw water×1 → CFcured fox fur×1
    Drying Rack 150s T1 3 kJ
    Scrape, bark-cure and work a fox pelt until the fur stays soft and supple.
    tex_cure_fur_fox
    CWcured wolf fur×1
    WPwolf pelt×1 CBcurled bark strip×2 RWraw water×1 → CWcured wolf fur×1
    Drying Rack 150s T1 3 kJ
    Scrape, bark-cure and work a wolf pelt into a thick, warm fur.
    tex_cure_fur_wolf
    WWwashed wool×1
    RWraw wool×1 RWraw water×1 → WWwashed wool×1
    Drying Rack 60s T1 3 kJ
    Scour raw wool in warm water to wash out grease and dirt, then lay it out to dry.
    tex_wash_wool_raw
    WWwashed wool×1
    RWraw wool fleece×1 RWraw water×1 → WWwashed wool×1
    Drying Rack 60s T1 3 kJ
    Scour a raw fleece in warm water to wash out lanolin and dirt, then dry it.
    tex_wash_wool_fleece
    Earthen Kiln 3
    BLburned lime (quicklime)×1
    LNlimestone nodule×1 → BLburned lime (quicklime)×1
    Earthen Kiln
    Station: earthen_kiln_updraft • Byproduct: CO2
    itemdb_burned_lime_quicklime_earthen_kiln_updraft
    FCfired clay brick×4
    GCgreen clay brick×4 → FCfired clay brick×4
    Earthen Kiln 140s T2 120 kJ
    Fire dried green bricks near a thousand degrees until the clay sinters hard and water-resistant. Feed the kiln from its fuel slot.
    bld_fire_brick
    CRclay roof tile×4
    RCraw clay×2 LSloose sand×1 RWraw water×1 → CRclay roof tile×4
    Earthen Kiln 130s T2 110 kJ
    Press thin clay tiles with an overlap and fire them like brick. Heavy, but they outlast any thatch or shingle.
    bld_fire_roof_tile
    Electric Arc Furnace 27
    T6Ti-6Al-4V titanium alloy×1
    TItitanium ingot×3 AIaluminum ingot×1 → T6Ti-6Al-4V titanium alloy×1
    Electric Arc Furnace
    chemName=Ti-6Al-4V,formula=Ti6Al4V,elements=Ti:9|Al:1,obtain=var_alloying • Station: arc_furnace_electric
    itemdb_alloy_ti_6al_4v_arc_furnace_electric
    ZCzircaloy cladding alloy×4
    ZIzirconium ingot×4 TItin ingot×1 SIsteel ingot×1 → ZCzircaloy cladding alloy×4
    Electric Arc Furnace 120s T4 220 kJ
    Alloy nuclear-grade zirconium with ~1.5% tin and a trace of iron/chromium/nickel (carried in by the steel) to make Zircaloy -- the corrosion-resistant, neutron-transparent cladding that sheathes reactor fuel.
    zr_alloy_zircaloy
    ZCzircaloy cladding alloy×1
    ZSzirconium sponge×3 SIsteel ingot×1 → ZCzircaloy cladding alloy×1
    Electric Arc Furnace
    chemName=Zircaloy,formula=ZrSnFeCr,elements=Zr:9|Sn:1,obtain=vim_zircaloy • Station: arc_furnace_electric

    PPPAN precursor fiber×1 → CFcarbon fiber tow×1

    Electric Arc Furnace

    LAlithium-aluminosilicate melt×1
    SFsynthetic fused silica×4 ALalumina×1 LClithium carbonate (battery grade)×1 TDtitanium dioxide×1 → LAlithium-aluminosilicate melt×1
    Electric Arc Furnace 130s T6 230 kJ
    Melt fused silica with alumina and lithium carbonate, with a little titania as a nucleating agent, into a lithium-aluminosilicate glass blank.
    re_las_glass_melt
    IIiron ingot×1 SLslag×1
    SIsponge iron (DRI)×2 → IIiron ingot×1 SLslag×1
    Electric Arc Furnace 70s T3 140 kJ +1 byproduct
    Melt sponge iron down to nearly pure, very-low-carbon iron ingots -- electromagnet cores and clean alloying stock. Gangue floats off as a little slag.
    iron_melt_dri
    RKrefined kerosene propellant×1
    SKsynthetic kerosene×1 HGhydrogen gas×1 → RKrefined kerosene propellant×1
    Electric Arc Furnace
    Station: arc_furnace_electric

    OGoxygen gas×2 → DLdistilled LOX propellant×1
    Electric Arc Furnace
    Station: arc_furnace_electric
    itemdb_propellant_lox_distilled_arc_furnace_electric
    SSstainless steel ingot×1 SLslag×1
    SIsteel ingot×1 FEferrochrome×1 FEferronickel×1 → SSstainless steel ingot×1 SLslag×1
    Electric Arc Furnace +1 byproduct
    chemName=StainlessSteel,formula=FeNiCr,elements=Fe:10|Cr:2|Ni:1,obtain=aod_eaf • Station: arc_furnace_electric

    SIsteel ingot×3 SLslag×1
    SIsponge iron (DRI)×3 SSsteel scrap×1 BLburned lime (quicklime)×1 → SIsteel ingot×3 SLslag×1
    Electric Arc Furnace 150s T4 480 kJ +1 byproduct
    Electric-arc furnace: strike an arc onto a charge of sponge iron and scrap, melt and refine with a lime slag. The recycling route -- flexible, scrap-fed, and the basis for most specialty and low-CO2 steel.
    iron_eaf_steel
    TItitanium ingot×1
    TStitanium sponge×3 → TItitanium ingot×1
    Electric Arc Furnace
    chemName=Titanium,formula=Ti,elements=Ti:1,obtain=vacuum_arc_remelt • Station: arc_furnace_electric

    TTtitanium tetrachloride×1 SMsodium metal×4 → TStitanium sponge×1 RHraw halite ore×1

    chemName=Titanium,formula=Ti,elements=Ti:1,obtain=hunter_process • Station: arc_furnace_electric

    TTtitanium tetrachloride×1 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1
    RIraw ilmenite ore×2 CGchlorine gas×1 CFcoke fuel×2 → TTtitanium tetrachloride×1 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1
    Electric Arc Furnace +1 byproduct
    chemName=TitaniumTetrachloride,formula=TiCl4,elements=Ti:1|Cl:4,obtain=ilmenite_chlorination • Station: arc_furnace_electric

    TCtungsten carbide powder×3
    TMtungsten metal powder×3 CHcharcoal×1 → TCtungsten carbide powder×3
    Electric Arc Furnace 130s T5 220 kJ
    Heat tungsten powder with carbon until it takes up carbon as tungsten carbide: W + C -> WC. Near diamond-hard, but still just a powder -- it needs a binder to become a tool.
    w_carburize_wc

    RWraw wolframite ore×1 SHsodium hydroxide×1 → TTtungsten trioxide×1 SLslag×1

    SCstaged-combustion kerolox engine×1
    SCstaged-combustion preburner×1 HPhigh-pressure turbopump×1 PIpintle injector×1 RTregen thrust chamber×1 TTTEA-TEB igniter×1 MPmain propellant valve×2 G/gimbal / TVC assembly×1 CBcopper bus bar×4 BPboiler pressure sensor×2 ISInconel superalloy ingot×1 → SCstaged-combustion kerolox engine×1

    Electric Machine Shop 80s T6 140 kJ

    Electric Machine Shop 380s T6 460 kJ

    Electric Machine Shop 470s T6 520 kJ
    GROUPED PATH: one machining cell mills inducer, impellers, rotor, bearings and seals from raw stock and assembles the turbopump in a single long pass.

    Electric Machine Shop
