    RSrolled steel plate×1 PRpneumatic regulator×1 MFmechanical face seal×1 → MPmain propellant valve×1
    Electric Machine Shop 70s T6 130 kJ
    Build a helium-piloted main propellant valve sealed by a mechanical face seal.
    re_valve_main
    WSwafer slicing & polishing line×1
    RSrolled steel beam×1 RSrolled steel plate×1 SDsmall DC motor×1 LSline speed sensor×1 → WSwafer slicing & polishing line×1
    Electric Machine Shop
    Station: machine shop (electric)
    itemdb_wafer_fab_line_machine_shop_electric
    YPYellowcake Precipitator×1
    SSstainless steel sheet×4 SDsmall DC motor×1 TSturned steel shaft×1 TLtank level sensor×1 → YPYellowcake Precipitator×1
    Electric Machine Shop
    Station: machine shop (electric)
    itemdb_yellowcake_precipitator_machine_shop_electric
    Electrolytic Refining Cell 2
    PGplatinum gauze catalyst×1
    ASanode slime×4 DCdrawn copper wire×1 → PGplatinum gauze catalyst×1
    Electrolytic Refining Cell 120s T5
    Recover platinum-group metal from the sludge under the copper refining cells (anode slime) and weave it into a screen. This screen later burns ammonia into nitric oxide without being used up.
    catalyst_pt_gauze_make
    CCcopper cathode×4 ASanode slime×1
    CAcopper anode×4 → CCcopper cathode×4 ASanode slime×1
    Electrolytic Refining Cell 600s T4 850 kJ +1 byproduct
    Electrolytic refining in CuSO4/H2SO4. Anode: Cu -> Cu2+ + 2e-; cathode: Cu2+ + 2e- -> Cu (99.99%). Au/Ag/Se/Te drop as anode slime.
    cu_electrorefine
    Electrowinning Cell 4
    CCcobalt cathode×1 SLslag×1
    PCpurified cobalt solution×1 DPDC power charge×2 → CCcobalt cathode×1 SLslag×1
    Electrowinning Cell 120s T4 200 kJ +1 byproduct
    Electrowin bright cobalt metal onto starter sheets -- the pure cathode that finally lets the carbide insert use its correct binder.
    co_electrowin
    CCcopper cathode×2 SAsulfuric acid×1 OGoxygen gas×1
    RCrich copper electrolyte×2 → CCcopper cathode×2 SAsulfuric acid×1 OGoxygen gas×1
    Electrowinning Cell 90s T3 400 kJ +2 byproducts
    Pass current from inert lead-alloy anodes to stainless cathodes: Cu(2+) + 2 e- -> Cu(0) plates ~99.99% cathode copper, while 2 H2O -> O2 + 4 H+ + 4 e- at the anode. Net 2 CuSO4 + 2 H2O -> 2 Cu + O2 + 2 H2SO4 -- the regenerated acid goes back to stripping and leaching, closing the loop. Energy-hungry, but no smelting and no SO2.
    cu_electrowin
    CMcadmium metal×1
    ZSzinc sulfate liquor×2 ZIzinc ingot×1 → CMcadmium metal×1
    Electrowinning Cell 120.0s 200.0 kJ
    time=120,energyamount=200,energyunit=kJ,categoryid=extraction • Station: electrowinning_cell
    itemdb_metal_cadmium_electrowinning_cell
    NCnickel cathode×1 SLslag×1
    NOnickel oxide sinter×1 DPDC power charge×2 → NCnickel cathode×1 SLslag×1
    Electrowinning Cell 120s T4 200 kJ +1 byproduct
    Dissolve and electrowin the nickel oxide onto starter sheets: pure nickel cathode plates out, gangue drops as anode slag. The same cathode the laterite route eventually reaches -- and the feed for Inconel.
    nis_electrowin
    Engine Lathe 2
    CIcentrifugal impeller×1
    ISInconel superalloy ingot×1 TAtitanium alloy plate×1 → CIcentrifugal impeller×1
    Engine Lathe 80s T6 150 kJ
    Mill a centrifugal pump impeller (one for fuel, one for oxidizer).
    re_pump_impeller
    PIpump inducer×1
    TAtitanium alloy plate×1 TSturned steel shaft×1 → PIpump inducer×1
    Engine Lathe 70s T6 130 kJ
    Turn a titanium inlet inducer screw to suppress cavitation ahead of the impeller.
    re_pump_inducer
    Fire-Refining Furnace 1
    SLsoftened lead×2 SZsilver-zinc crust×1
    CLcrude lead bullion×2 ZIzinc ingot×1 → SLsoftened lead×2 SZsilver-zinc crust×1
    Fire-Refining Furnace 130s T3 635 kJ +1 byproduct
    The Parkes process: stir zinc into the molten bullion. Silver is far more soluble in zinc than in lead, so it floats out as a zinc-silver crust that is skimmed off, leaving softened lead. (This is why lead refining quietly consumes zinc.)
    pb_parkes_desilver
    Flash Smelting Furnace 1
    NFnickel furnace matte×1 SLslag×1 SDsulfur dioxide gas×1
    NCnickel concentrate×2 RSraw silica ore×1 OGoxygen gas×1 → NFnickel furnace matte×1 SLslag×1 SDsulfur dioxide gas×1
    Flash Smelting Furnace 110s T3 385 kJ +2 byproducts
    Flash-smelt the concentrate in an oxygen-enriched blast: the sulfide burns its own fuel (autogenous), iron slags off with silica flux, and the sulfur leaves as SO2 -- piped straight to the contact acid plant. The molten matte is tapped below.
    nis_flash_smelt
    Fluidized Bed Chlorinator 6
    ACallyl chloride×1 HAhydrochloric acid gas×1
    PRpropylene×1 CGchlorine gas×1 → ACallyl chloride×1 HAhydrochloric acid gas×1
    Fluidized Bed Chlorinator 60s T5 +1 byproduct
    Chlorinate propylene hot (~500C) so the chlorine substitutes onto the methyl group instead of adding across the double bond, giving allyl chloride and releasing HCl.
    allyl_chloride_chlorinate
    EDethylene dichloride×1
    ETethylene×1 CGchlorine gas×1 → EDethylene dichloride×1
    Fluidized Bed Chlorinator 45s T5
    Add chlorine across ethylene to make ethylene dichloride (direct chlorination).
    edc_chlorinate
    CTcrude titanium tetrachloride×2 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1
    SRsynthetic rutile×2 CFcoke fuel×1 CGchlorine gas×2 → CTcrude titanium tetrachloride×2 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1
    Fluidized Bed Chlorinator 120s T4 200 kJ +1 byproduct
    Fluidise the rutile with coke and blow chlorine through at ~1000C: TiO2 + C + 2 Cl2 -> TiCl4 + CO2. The carbon grabs the oxygen, the chlorine grabs the titanium, and "tickle" boils off as a vapour.
    ti_chlorinate_rutile
    TRtrichlorosilane×1 HGhydrogen gas×1
    MGmetallurgical-grade silicon×1 HAhydrochloric acid gas×3 → TRtrichlorosilane×1 HGhydrogen gas×1
    Fluidized Bed Chlorinator 70s T4 90 kJ +1 byproduct
    Hydrochlorinate metallurgical silicon in a fluidised bed: Si + 3 HCl -> SiHCl3 + H2. The point is to turn solid silicon into a volatile liquid that can be fractionally distilled to a purity no solid could ever reach.
    si_make_trichlorosilane
    TRtrichlorosilane×4
    STsilicon tetrachloride×3 MGmetallurgical-grade silicon×1 HGhydrogen gas×2 → TRtrichlorosilane×4
    Fluidized Bed Chlorinator 80s T4 110 kJ
    Redistribute silicon tetrachloride back into useful trichlorosilane: 3 SiCl4 + Si + 2 H2 -> 4 SiHCl3. This recycles the SiCl4 thrown off by the Siemens reactor (and consumes the zirconium plant's chlorinator byproduct) instead of dumping it.
    si_redistribute_sicl4
    CZcrude zirconium tetrachloride×2 STsilicon tetrachloride×1 CDcarbon dioxide gas×2
    RZraw zircon ore×2 CFcoke fuel×2 CGchlorine gas×4 → CZcrude zirconium tetrachloride×2 STsilicon tetrachloride×1 CDcarbon dioxide gas×2
    Fluidized Bed Chlorinator 130s T4 210 kJ +2 byproducts
    Fluidise zircon sand with coke and blow chlorine through at ~1200C: ZrSiO4 + 2 C + 4 Cl2 -> ZrCl4 + SiCl4 + 2 CO2. The silicate splits in two -- both chlorides boil off and are condensed separately. Carbon takes the oxygen, chlorine takes the metals.
    zr_carbochlorinate
    Fluoride Digestion Reactor 2
    BTboron trifluoride×2
    BOboric oxide (B2O3)×1 HAhydrofluoric acid×6 → BTboron trifluoride×2
    Fluoride Digestion Reactor 90s T6 150 kJ
    Digest boric oxide (calcined from borax) in hydrofluoric acid to release boron trifluoride gas.
    re_boron_trifluoride
    CNcrude Nb/Ta fluoride liquor×2 TAtailings×1
    RCraw columbite ore×2 HAhydrofluoric acid×4 → CNcrude Nb/Ta fluoride liquor×2 TAtailings×1
    Fluoride Digestion Reactor 120s T5 110 kJ +1 byproduct
    Digest crushed columbite in hot hydrofluoric acid. Both niobium and tantalum dissolve together as fluoro-complexes, leaving the gangue as tailings. HF is the only acid that will touch these oxides.
    nb_digest_hf
    Fluorine Cell 1
    FGfluorine gas×1 HGhydrogen gas×1
    HAhydrofluoric acid×2 → FGfluorine gas×1 HGhydrogen gas×1
    Fluorine Cell 90s T6 220 kJ +1 byproduct
    Electrolyse molten HF/KF: 2 HF -> H2 + F2. The only route to elemental fluorine on Earth -- nothing chemical can pry it loose. Hungry, dangerous, and necessary for the hexafluoride.
    u_make_fluorine
    Forge Hearth 6
    CHCupellation Hearth×1
    FRfireclay refractory brick×6 SCsmall clay crucible×2 HIhammered iron plate×2 CTclay tuyere×1 → CHCupellation Hearth×1
    Forge Hearth
    Station: forge hearth
    itemdb_cupellation_hearth_forge_hearth
    DSDRI Shaft Furnace×1
    FRfireclay refractory brick×8 HIhammered iron plate×4 CTclay tuyere×2 HFhand-forged iron rivet×6 → DSDRI Shaft Furnace×1
    Forge Hearth
    Station: forge hearth
    itemdb_dri_shaft_furnace_forge_hearth
    RFReverberatory Furnace×1
    FRfireclay refractory brick×10 FCfired clay brick×4 HIhammered iron plate×2 CTclay tuyere×2 → RFReverberatory Furnace×1
    Forge Hearth
    Station: forge hearth
    itemdb_reverberatory_furnace_forge_hearth
    HBhafted bronze knife×1
    BIbronze ingot×1 WHwood haft×1 ROrope×1 → HBhafted bronze knife×1
    Forge Hearth
    tier=2 • Station: forge_hearth
    itemdb_tool_bronze_knife_hafted_forge_hearth
    HIhafted iron knife×1
    IIiron ingot×1 WHwood haft×1 ROrope×1 → HIhafted iron knife×1
    Forge Hearth
    tier=3 • Station: forge_hearth
    itemdb_tool_iron_knife_hafted_forge_hearth
    ZDZinc Distillation Retort×1
    SCsmall clay crucible×6 FRfireclay refractory brick×6 HIhammered iron plate×2 CTclay tuyere×1 → ZDZinc Distillation Retort×1
    Forge Hearth
    Station: forge hearth
    itemdb_zinc_distillation_retort_forge_hearth
    Froth Flotation Cell 2
    NCnickel concentrate×2 TAtailings×1
    RPraw pentlandite ore×3 → NCnickel concentrate×2 TAtailings×1
    Froth Flotation Cell 70s T2 50 kJ +1 byproduct
    Grind and float pentlandite: sulfide grains ride the froth while silicate gangue sinks, upgrading the ore into a nickel sulfide concentrate before any heat is spent.
    nis_froth_flotation
    MOmuriate of potash (KCl)×2 SLsalt lick×1
    RSraw sylvite ore×3 → MOmuriate of potash (KCl)×2 SLsalt lick×1
    Froth Flotation Cell 80s T3 55 kJ +1 byproduct
    Float sylvinite with an amine collector: the KCl rides the froth as muriate of potash while the common salt (NaCl) sinks and is recovered as a salable byproduct rather than waste.
    k_froth_flotation
    Fuel Rod Assembly Line 2
    HChafnium control rod×2
    HShafnium sponge×2 RSrolled steel plate×1 → HChafnium control rod×2
    Fuel Rod Assembly Line 80s T7 90 kJ
    Clad hafnium metal in a steel sheath to make a control rod. Hafnium absorbs neutrons greedily -- the same property that made it a poison to remove from zirconium makes it the perfect throttle for the core.
    u_make_control_rod
    EUenriched uranium fuel rod×1
    UDuranium dioxide pellet×8 ZCzircaloy cladding alloy×2 → EUenriched uranium fuel rod×1
    Fuel Rod Assembly Line 90s T7 110 kJ
    Stack the pellets in a Zircaloy cladding tube and seal it: this is why zirconium was refined to be neutron-transparent. The finished fuel rod -- pellets + cladding -- is the bridge between the uranium and zirconium chains.
    u_load_fuel_rod
    Gas Centrifuge Cascade 1
    EUenriched UF6 (LEU)×1 DUdepleted UF6 tails×4
    UHuranium hexafluoride×5 → EUenriched UF6 (LEU)×1 DUdepleted UF6 tails×4
    Gas Centrifuge Cascade 260s T7 480 kJ +1 byproduct
    Spin UF6 through a cascade of gas centrifuges. Natural uranium is 0.7% U-235; you skim a little up to ~4% reactor-grade LEU and are left with a large depleted-uranium tails stream. Most of the feed leaves as tails -- that is what "separative work" really costs.
    u_enrich_centrifuge
    Gas Compressor 2
    CAcompressed air×10
    Gas Compressor 5s T1
    Draw in and filter atmospheric air. Free and inexhaustible -- the feedstock for everything downstream of the ASU.
    gather_air_raw
    HChelium cylinder×1
    BGbasic gas cylinder×1 HGhelium gas×1 → HChelium cylinder×1
    Gas Compressor 30s T4 40 kJ
    Compress purified helium into a cylinder for storage and transport. Seal it well -- helium leaks through gaskets that hold any other gas.
    he_fill_cylinder
    Gas Well Derrick 1
    RNraw natural gas×2
    Gas Well Derrick 60s T4 40 kJ
    Drill a gas well and bring sour natural gas up the derrick. What comes up is mostly methane, with CO2, hydrogen sulfide and -- in helium-rich fields -- a trace of helium along for the ride.
    ng_gather_natural_gas
    Gathered from the world 35
    SAsulfuric acid×1
    chemName=SulfuricAcid,formula=H2SO4,elements=H:2|S:1|O:4,obtain=contact_process • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- contact_process
    world_acid_sulfuric
    ACacrylonitrile×1
    chemName=Acrylonitrile,formula=C3H3N,elements=C:3|H:3|N:1,obtain=sohio_process • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- sohio_process
    world_acrylonitrile
    GCglass cell×1
    obtain=craft_or_find • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- craft_or_find
    world_cell_glass
    MCmetal cell×1
    obtain=craft_or_find • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- craft_or_find
    world_cell_metal
    BCblister copper×1
    chemName=BlisterCopper,formula=Cu,elements=Cu:1,purity=98pct,obtain=peirce_smith • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- peirce_smith
    world_copper_blister
    COcork oak bark×1
    obtain=cork_oak_grove • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- cork_oak_grove
    world_cork_oak_bark
    BGbasic gas cylinder×1
    obtain=craft_or_find • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- craft_or_find
    world_cylinder_gas_basic
    ERepoxy resin×1
    chemName=EpoxyResin,obtain=phenol_epichlorohydrin • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- phenol_epichlorohydrin
    world_epoxy_resin
    FEferrochrome×1
    chemName=Ferrochrome,formula=FeCr,elements=Fe:1|Cr:2,obtain=chromite_carbothermic • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- chromite_carbothermic
    world_ferrochrome
    FEferronickel×1
    chemName=Ferronickel,formula=FeNi,elements=Fe:1|Ni:1,obtain=pentlandite_arc_smelt • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- pentlandite_arc_smelt
    world_ferronickel
    AGammonia gas×1
    chemName=Ammonia,formula=NH3,elements=N:1|H:3,obtain=haber_bosch • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- haber_bosch
    world_gas_ammonia
    CGchlorine gas×1
    chemName=Chlorine,formula=Cl2,elements=Cl:2,obtain=chlor_alkali_cell • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- chlor_alkali_cell
    world_gas_chlorine
    CDcarbon dioxide gas×1
    chemName=CarbonDioxide,formula=CO2,elements=C:1|O:2,obtain=byproduct_smelting • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- byproduct_smelting
    world_gas_co2
    HGhelium gas×1
    chemName=Helium,formula=He,elements=He:1,obtain=natural_gas_processing • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- natural_gas_processing
    world_gas_helium
    HGhydrogen gas×1
    chemName=Hydrogen,formula=H2,elements=H:2,obtain=electrolysis_or_reforming • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- electrolysis_or_reforming
    world_gas_hydrogen
    NGnitrogen gas×1
    chemName=Nitrogen,formula=N2,elements=N:2,obtain=air_separation • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- air_separation
    world_gas_nitrogen
    OGoxygen gas×1
    chemName=Oxygen,formula=O2,elements=O:2,obtain=air_separation_or_electrolysis • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- air_separation_or_electrolysis
    world_gas_oxygen
    NCnickel cathode×1
    chemName=Nickel,formula=Ni,elements=Ni:1,obtain=mond_or_electrowin • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- mond_or_electrowin
    world_nickel_cathode
    EGelectronic-grade polysilicon×1
    chemName=Polysilicon,formula=Si,elements=Si:1,purity=9N,obtain=siemens_cvd • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- siemens_cvd
    world_polysilicon_9n
    BOborax×1
    obtain=rock_outcrop • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- rock_outcrop
    world_raw_ore_borax
    OCoxidized copper ore×3
    25s T1
    Mine the weathered, brightly coloured oxide cap of a copper deposit. The easy, smelter-free entry to copper -- it leaches instead of roasting, so it pairs with the acid plant rather than the furnace.
    gather_ore_copper_oxide
    DOdolomite×1
    obtain=rock_outcrop • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- rock_outcrop
    world_raw_ore_dolomite
    NLnickel laterite (saprolite)×3
    30s T2
    Strip-mine the soft, deeply weathered nickel laterite. Low grade but enormous and shallow -- no shaft, no concentration, straight to the kiln.
    gather_ore_nickel_laterite
    PRphosphate rock×2
    Found in the world: Dig phosphate rock from pale sedimentary beds and weathered outcrops.
    T3
    world_raw_ore_phosphate
    RSrefining slag×1
    chemName=RefiningSlag,obtain=metal_refining • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- metal_refining
    world_refining_slag
    SLsalt lick×1
    obtain=mineral_deposit,formula=NaCl,chemName=SodiumChloride • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- mineral_deposit
    world_salt
    SSsteel scrap×2
    20s T1
    Collect and shear scrap steel from the world. Real arc-furnace steelmaking is fundamentally a recycling process -- scrap is a primary feedstock, not a byproduct.
    gather_scrap_steel
    CSCzochralski silicon ingot×1
    chemName=Silicon,formula=Si,elements=Si:1,purity=9N,obtain=czochralski_pull • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- czochralski_pull
    world_silicon_ingot_czochralski
    MGmetallurgical-grade silicon×1
    chemName=Silicon,formula=Si,elements=Si:1,purity=98pct,obtain=carbothermic_arc • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- carbothermic_arc
    world_silicon_mg_grade
    PSpolished silicon wafer×1
    chemName=Silicon,formula=Si,elements=Si:1,obtain=czochralski_cmp • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- czochralski_cmp
    world_silicon_wafer_polished
    SHsodium hydroxide×1
    chemName=SodiumHydroxide,formula=NaOH,elements=Na:1|O:1|H:1,obtain=chlor_alkali_process • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- chlor_alkali_process
    world_sodium_hydroxide
    TStoken: solid-state tier complete×1
    obtain=quest_reward • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- quest_reward
    world_token_solid_state_tier_complete
    TRtrichlorosilane×1
    chemName=Trichlorosilane,formula=SiHCl3,elements=Si:1|H:1|Cl:3,obtain=mgsi_hcl_route • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- mgsi_hcl_route
    world_trichlorosilane
    RGrefined gypsum×1
    chemName=Gypsum,formula=CaSO4,elements=Ca:1|S:1|O:4,obtain=hf_synthesis_byproduct • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- hf_synthesis_byproduct
    world_waste_gypsum_refined
    RWraw water×1
    chemName=WaterRaw,formula=H2O,elements=H:2|O:1,obtain=fill_container_at_water_source,meta=water_raw • ★ FLOOR PICKUP ITEM ★ -- fill_container_at_water_source
    world_water_raw
    Glass Bench 4
    GCglass cell×2
    BGborosilicate glass tube×2 → GCglass cell×2
    Glass Bench 45s T3 60 kJ
    Blow and seal borosilicate tube into a glass cell -- inert to acids and thermal shock, for corrosive contents you want to see.
    cell_form_glass
    FGflat glass sheet×2
    RSraw silica ore×3 SSsodium sulfate×1 CHcharcoal×2 → FGflat glass sheet×2
    Glass Bench
    Station: glass_bench
    itemdb_glass_sheet_flat_glass_bench
    BGborosilicate glass tube×4
    RSraw silica ore×2 BOborax×1 CHcharcoal×2 → BGborosilicate glass tube×4
    Glass Bench
    Station: glass_bench
    itemdb_glass_tube_glass_bench
    GFglass-fiber roving×3
    RSraw silica ore×2 BOborax×1 CHcharcoal×1 → GFglass-fiber roving×3
    Glass Bench 60s T6 130 kJ
    Melt a borosilicate batch and draw it through fine bushings into continuous glass-fiber roving.
    re_glassfiber_roving
    Haber-Bosch Reactor 4
    AGammonia gas×2
    NGnitrogen gas×1 HGhydrogen gas×3 → AGammonia gas×2
    Haber-Bosch Reactor 150s T4 320 kJ
    Fix nitrogen: N2 + 3 H2 -> 2 NH3 over a promoted-iron catalyst at ~450C and >100 bar. Only a fraction converts per pass, so the unreacted gas is recycled. Hydrogen comes from the chlor-alkali cell, nitrogen from the ASU -- the two new gas plants closing into one.
    hb_synthesize_ammonia
    CMcarbon monoxide×1 HGhydrogen gas×3
    MGmethane gas×1 SCsteam charge×1 → CMcarbon monoxide×1 HGhydrogen gas×3
    Haber-Bosch Reactor 90s T6 170 kJ +1 byproduct
    Steam-reform methane into synthesis gas: carbon monoxide plus hydrogen.
    re_syngas_reform
    HYhydrazine×1
    AGammonia gas×2 → HYhydrazine×1
    Haber-Bosch Reactor 90s T6 160 kJ
    Partially oxidize ammonia into hydrazine (Raschig / ketazine route, simplified).
    re_hydrazine
    MEmethanol×1
    CMcarbon monoxide×1 HGhydrogen gas×2 → MEmethanol×1
    Haber-Bosch Reactor 90s T6 150 kJ
    Catalytically combine carbon monoxide with hydrogen under pressure into methanol.
    re_methanol
    Hall-Heroult Cell 1
    AIaluminum ingot×2 CDcarbon dioxide gas×2
    ALalumina×2 PCprebaked carbon anode×1 CRcryolite×1 → AIaluminum ingot×2 CDcarbon dioxide gas×2
    Hall-Heroult Cell 200s T4 600 kJ +1 byproduct
    Dissolve the alumina in a molten cryolite bath (~960 C) and drive a brutal DC current through it. Oxygen migrates to the carbon anode and burns it away while pure aluminum pools at the cathode: 2 Al2O3 + 3 C -> 4 Al + 3 CO2. At ~17,000 kWh per tonne this is the most energy-hungry single step in the whole tech tree -- aluminum is, quite literally, solid electricity.
    al_hall_heroult_smelt
    Heap Leach Pad 6
    CScobalt sulfate liquor×2 TAtailings×1
    RCroasted cobalt oxide×2 SAsulfuric acid×2 → CScobalt sulfate liquor×2 TAtailings×1
    Heap Leach Pad 120s T4 90 kJ +1 byproduct
    Leach the roasted oxide in sulfuric acid to a pink cobalt sulfate liquor, leaving the inert gangue as tailings.
    co_leach
    PLpregnant leach solution (PLS)×3 TAtailings×1
    COcrushed oxide copper ore×3 SAsulfuric acid×2 → PLpregnant leach solution (PLS)×3 TAtailings×1
    Heap Leach Pad 120s T2 20 kJ +1 byproduct
    Stack the ore on a lined pad and trickle dilute sulfuric acid through it. Oxides dissolve with NO oxidant needed: CuO + H2SO4 -> CuSO4 + H2O; malachite Cu2CO3(OH)2 + 2 H2SO4 -> 2 CuSO4 + 3 H2O + CO2. Carbonate/silicate gangue is the real acid sink. Copper-laden PLS drains off the base; spent tailings stay behind.
    cu_heap_leach_oxide
    LSlithium sulfate liquor×2 TAtailings×1
    CLcrude lithium sulfate×2 BLburned lime (quicklime)×1 → LSlithium sulfate liquor×2 TAtailings×1
    Heap Leach Pad 120s T4 90 kJ +1 byproduct
    Water-leach the bake and add lime to precipitate iron and aluminium, leaving a clean lithium sulfate liquor.
    li_leach_purify
    PCPGM concentrate×1
    ASanode slime×2 SAsulfuric acid×1 → PCPGM concentrate×1
    Heap Leach Pad 110s T6 150 kJ
    Acid-leach copper-tankhouse anode slime to strip base metals, leaving a platinum-group + gold concentrate.
    re_pgm_concentrate
    UPuranium pregnant liquor×3 TAtailings×2
    RUraw uraninite ore×3 SAsulfuric acid×2 → UPuranium pregnant liquor×3 TAtailings×2
    Heap Leach Pad 140s T6 120 kJ +1 byproduct
    Irrigate crushed uraninite with sulfuric acid: the uranium dissolves into a pregnant liquor, the spent rock is left as tailings. The first and dirtiest step of milling.
    u_acid_leach
    ZSzinc sulfate liquor×2 TAtailings×1
    ZOzinc oxide calcine×2 SAsulfuric acid×2 → ZSzinc sulfate liquor×2 TAtailings×1
    Heap Leach Pad 120s T3 80 kJ +1 byproduct
    Leach the calcine in sulfuric acid to a zinc sulfate liquor, leaving the iron-rich residue behind.
    zn_leach
    Helium Purifier 1
    HGhelium gas×1
    CHcrude helium×2 → HGhelium gas×1
    Helium Purifier 90s T5 130 kJ
    Polish crude helium to 99.999% by pressure-swing adsorption and a final cold trap. Some is lost to the tails -- helium is too fugitive to chase the last fraction of a percent cheaply.
    ng_purify_helium
    Hf-Zr Separation Column 1
    ZTzirconium tetrachloride×3 HThafnium tetrachloride×1
    CZcrude zirconium tetrachloride×3 → ZTzirconium tetrachloride×3 HThafnium tetrachloride×1
    Hf-Zr Separation Column 110s T4 150 kJ +1 byproduct
    The keystone step: pull the chemically-identical hafnium twin out of the zirconium chloride by extractive distillation / solvent extraction, dropping hafnium below ~100 ppm. This is the single hardest separation in routine metallurgy -- and the only reason reactor-grade zirconium exists. The hafnium leaves as its own chloride.
    zr_separate_hafnium
    Hunter Reduction Retort 3
    HShafnium sponge×2 RHraw halite ore×8
    HThafnium tetrachloride×2 SMsodium metal×8 → HShafnium sponge×2 RHraw halite ore×8
    Hunter Reduction Retort 130s T6 635 kJ +1 byproduct
    Sodium-reduce the hafnium tetrachloride split out during zirconium refining: HfCl4 + 4 Na -> Hf + 4 NaCl. Gives the orphaned hafnium byproduct a purpose and regenerates salt, exactly like the zirconium Hunter step.
    u_reduce_hafnium_sponge
    TStitanium sponge×1 RHraw halite ore×4
    TTtitanium tetrachloride×1 SMsodium metal×4 → TStitanium sponge×1 RHraw halite ore×4
    Hunter Reduction Retort 200s T4 980 kJ +1 byproduct
    Reduce purified TiCl4 with molten sodium in a sealed, inert retort: TiCl4 + 4 Na -> Ti + 4 NaCl. The titanium grows as a porous sponge and the salt byproduct loops straight back to the brine plant. (The modern Kroll process uses magnesium instead; we run Hunter because sodium is what our plant makes.)
    ti_hunter_reduce
    ZSzirconium sponge×1 RHraw halite ore×4
    ZTzirconium tetrachloride×1 SMsodium metal×4 → ZSzirconium sponge×1 RHraw halite ore×4
    Hunter Reduction Retort 200s T4 980 kJ +1 byproduct
    Reduce the purified ZrCl4 with molten sodium in a sealed, inert retort: ZrCl4 + 4 Na -> Zr + 4 NaCl. Zirconium grows as a porous sponge; the salt loops straight back to the brine plant. (Modern Kroll uses magnesium -- same idea, ZrCl4 + 2 Mg -> Zr + 2 MgCl2 -- but sodium is what our plant makes.)
    zr_kroll_reduce
    Hydrogen Reduction Furnace 3
    ANaniline×1
    NInitrobenzene×1 HGhydrogen gas×3 → ANaniline×1
    Hydrogen Reduction Furnace 90s T6 150 kJ
    Hydrogenate nitrobenzene to the aromatic amine aniline.
    re_aniline
    ISiridium sponge×1
    AHammonium hexachloroiridate×1 HGhydrogen gas×2 → ISiridium sponge×1
    Hydrogen Reduction Furnace 120s T6 200 kJ
    Ignite the salt in hydrogen to drive off ammonia and chlorine, leaving a sponge of metallic iridium.
    re_iridium_sponge
    TMtungsten metal powder×1 RWraw water×3
    TTtungsten trioxide×1 HGhydrogen gas×3 → TMtungsten metal powder×1 RWraw water×3
    Hydrogen Reduction Furnace 120s T4 180 kJ +1 byproduct
    Reduce the oxide to metal in flowing hydrogen: WO3 + 3 H2 -> W + 3 H2O. The product is a fine powder -- and powder is the only practical form, because tungsten melts at 3422 C and cannot be cast.
    w_reduce_powder
    Knapping Station 6
    FAflint arrowhead×2
    FFflint flake×1 → FAflint arrowhead×2
    Knapping Station 25s T1 5 kJ
    Bifacially work a flake into a tipped, barbed projectile point for arrows and darts.
    fl_make_arrowhead
    KFknapped flint blade×1
    FFflint flake×2 → KFknapped flint blade×1
    Knapping Station 40s T1 8 kJ
    Pressure-retouch a flake down one edge into a straight, durable knife/scraper blade.
    fl_knap_blade
    FFflint flake×3 SFstone flakes×1
    RFraw flint×2 → FFflint flake×3 SFstone flakes×1
    Knapping Station 30s T1 6 kJ +1 byproduct
    Strike a flint core with a hammerstone: conchoidal fracture shears off sharp flakes. The blunt shatter and trimming chips fall away as stone debitage -- a salvage byproduct, not loss.
    fl_knap_flint
    ODobsidian dart point×2
    OFobsidian flake×1 → ODobsidian dart point×2
    Knapping Station 25s T1 5 kJ
    Bifacially pressure-flake a flake into a razor projectile point for arrows and atlatl darts.
    ob_make_arrowhead
    PFpressure-flaked obsidian blade×1
    OFobsidian flake×2 CWconstruction wood×1 → PFpressure-flaked obsidian blade×1
    Knapping Station 50s T2 10 kJ
    Pressure-flake a flake with a wood/antler billet, pressing off tiny chips to refine the edge to ~3 nm -- sharper than surgical steel. The billet is consumed.
    ob_pressure_blade
    OFobsidian flake×3 SFstone flakes×1
    ROraw obsidian×2 → OFobsidian flake×3 SFstone flakes×1
    Knapping Station 30s T1 6 kJ +1 byproduct
    Strike an obsidian core: the glass fractures conchoidally into flakes already keener than flint. Shatter and trim chips fall away as stone debitage.
    ob_knap_obsidian
    Lab Vacuum Pump 1
    HNhelium-neon lasing gas×1
    HGhelium gas×3 NEneon×1 → HNhelium-neon lasing gas×1
    Lab Vacuum Pump 50s T6 70 kJ
    Blend helium and neon to the precise low-pressure ratio of a helium-neon laser fill.
    re_he_ne_fill
    Lead Chamber (NOx) 2
    SAsulfuric acid×1
    CAchamber acid (dilute H2SO4)×2 → SAsulfuric acid×1
    Lead Chamber (NOx) 35s T3 75 kJ
    Boil chamber acid up in the Glover tower to drive off water and lift it toward concentrated strength. Tier-3 path to H2SO4 before the vanadium contact plant is available.
    acid_concentrate_chamber
    CAchamber acid (dilute H2SO4)×2
    SDsulfur dioxide gas×3 RWraw water×1 → CAchamber acid (dilute H2SO4)×2
    Lead Chamber (NOx) 40s T3 85 kJ
    The pre-Contact route (Roebuck, 1746): SO2 oxidised by moist air with recycled nitrogen oxides (NOx) as catalyst inside lead-lined chambers. Cheap and robust but caps out near 65-78% acid. NOx is recovered in the Gay-Lussac/Glover towers and reused.
    acid_lead_chamber
    Lever Press 1
    PApaper×3
    PPpaper pulp×2 RWraw water×1 → PApaper×3
    Lever Press 90s T1 120 kJ
    Dip a screen into the pulp vat, couch the wet sheet, and press out the water on the lever press; the sheet dries to finished paper.
    ppr_press_sheet
    Lithium Acid Roast Kiln 2
    CLcrude lithium sulfate×2 TAtailings×1
    BSbeta-spodumene×2 SAsulfuric acid×1 → CLcrude lithium sulfate×2 TAtailings×1
    Lithium Acid Roast Kiln 200s T4 840 kJ +1 byproduct
    Bake beta-spodumene with concentrated sulfuric acid at ~250 C: lithium ion-exchanges onto the sulfate, leaving the alumino-silicate skeleton as inert residue.
    li_acid_bake
    MRmixed rare-earth sulfate×2 TRthorium residue (radioactive)×1
    RErare earth concentrate×2 SAsulfuric acid×2 → MRmixed rare-earth sulfate×2 TRthorium residue (radioactive)×1
    Lithium Acid Roast Kiln 220s T5 925 kJ +1 byproduct
    Crack the concentrate by baking with sulfuric acid: the lanthanides go to soluble sulfates while the radioactive thorium drops out as a captured residue -- the dirty secret of every rare-earth mine.
    ree_acid_bake
    Magnesium Chloride Chlorinator 1
    AManhydrous magnesium chloride×1 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1
    MHmagnesium hydroxide×1 CGchlorine gas×1 CFcoke fuel×1 → AManhydrous magnesium chloride×1 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1
    Magnesium Chloride Chlorinator 130s T4 140 kJ +1 byproduct
    Carbo-chlorinate the hydroxide to bone-dry MgCl2 (carbon scavenges the oxygen as CO2, chlorine takes the magnesium). Anhydrous feed is essential -- any water hydrolyses it back to oxide.
    mg_chlorinate_mgcl2
    Magnesium Electrolysis Cell 2
    MImagnesium ingot×2 CGchlorine gas×1
    AManhydrous magnesium chloride×2 DPDC power charge×2 → MImagnesium ingot×2 CGchlorine gas×1
    Magnesium Electrolysis Cell 240s T4 420 kJ +1 byproduct
    Electrolyse molten MgCl2: magnesium metal collects at the cathode and chlorine gas comes off the anode -- piped straight back to the chlorinator, closing the chlorine loop just like the sodium loop in titanium.
    mg_electrolysis
    NMneodymium metal×2 OGoxygen gas×1
    NOneodymium oxide×2 DPDC power charge×3 → NMneodymium metal×2 OGoxygen gas×1
    Magnesium Electrolysis Cell 240s T6 460 kJ +1 byproduct
    Electrowin neodymium from a molten fluoride/oxide bath -- enormously energy-hungry, which is most of why rare-earth metals are expensive.
    ree_reduce_neodymium
    Magnesium Flux Remelt Furnace 1
    MImagnesium ingot×1
    MCmagnesium crown (crude)×2 → MImagnesium ingot×1
    Magnesium Flux Remelt Furnace 110s T4 100 kJ
    Remelt the crude crown under a protective salt flux (magnesium burns in air) and cast a clean magnesium ingot.
    mg_remelt_ingot
    Magnetic Separator 2
    IOiron ore concentrate×1 TAtailings×1
    CMcrushed magnetite×2 → IOiron ore concentrate×1 TAtailings×1
    Magnetic Separator 60s T2 35 kJ +1 byproduct
    Magnetite (Fe3O4) is strongly ferromagnetic, so low-intensity wet magnetic separation (LIMS) cleanly pulls it from silica gangue -- upgrading crushed ore to a ~65-69% Fe concentrate. The rejected gangue leaves as tailings.
    iron_magsep_magnetite
    IOiron ore concentrate×2 TAtailings×1
    CHcrushed hematite×3 CFcoke fuel×1 → IOiron ore concentrate×2 TAtailings×1
    Magnetic Separator 75s T2 45 kJ +1 byproduct
    Hematite (Fe2O3) is only weakly magnetic, so it is first magnetizing-roasted with a little reducing gas (3 Fe2O3 + CO -> 2 Fe3O4 + CO2) and THEN magnetically separated. Recovers a concentrate from low-grade hematite fines that gravity methods miss.
    iron_beneficiate_hematite
    Mannheim Furnace 1
    SOsulfate of potash (K2SO4)×2 HAhydrochloric acid gas×1
    MOmuriate of potash (KCl)×2 SAsulfuric acid×1 → SOsulfate of potash (K2SO4)×2 HAhydrochloric acid gas×1
    Mannheim Furnace 180s T4 170 kJ +1 byproduct
    Mannheim process: react KCl with sulfuric acid in a heated muffle -- 2 KCl + H2SO4 -> K2SO4 + 2 HCl. Yields chloride-free sulfate of potash and drives off hydrogen chloride gas as a genuine co-product.
    k_mannheim_sop
    Membrane Electrolysis Cell 2
    CGchlorine gas×1 DCdilute cell caustic×2 HGhydrogen gas×1
    PBpurified brine×2 → CGchlorine gas×1 DCdilute cell caustic×2 HGhydrogen gas×1
    Membrane Electrolysis Cell 120s T3 350 kJ +2 byproducts
    Electrolyse the purified brine across an ion-selective membrane: chlorine bubbles off the anode, hydrogen off the cathode, and sodium ions cross the membrane to leave caustic soda behind. 2 NaCl + 2 H2O -> Cl2 + H2 + 2 NaOH. The single biggest electricity draw outside the aluminium pots.
    ca_membrane_cell
    CPcaustic potash (KOH)×2 CGchlorine gas×1 HGhydrogen gas×1
    MOmuriate of potash (KCl)×2 RWraw water×2 DPDC power charge×2 → CPcaustic potash (KOH)×2 CGchlorine gas×1 HGhydrogen gas×1
    Membrane Electrolysis Cell 160s T4 420 kJ +2 byproducts
    Electrolyse a KCl brine in a membrane cell -- the potassium analogue of chlor-alkali: caustic potash at the cathode, chlorine at the anode, hydrogen alongside.
    k_membrane_koh
    Oleum Absorption Tower 5
    NAnitric acid×2 NOnitric oxide×1
    NDnitrogen dioxide×3 RWraw water×1 → NAnitric acid×2 NOnitric oxide×1
    Oleum Absorption Tower 50s T5 +1 byproduct
    Dissolve nitrogen dioxide in water to give nitric acid, releasing some nitric oxide that is piped back to the oxidizer (3 nitrogen dioxide + 1 water makes 2 nitric acid + 1 nitric oxide).
    ostwald_no2_absorb
    SAsulfuric acid×3
    O(oleum (fuming sulfuric acid)×2 RWraw water×1 → SAsulfuric acid×3
    Oleum Absorption Tower 20s T4 12 kJ
    Bleed oleum down with a metered water add: H2S2O7 + H2O -> 2 H2SO4. Yields concentrated ~98% acid; part is drawn off as product, part recycled back to the absorption tower.
    acid_dilute_oleum
    BEbenzene×1 PIpitch×1
    CTcoal tar×2 → BEbenzene×1 PIpitch×1
    Oleum Absorption Tower 80s T5 +1 byproduct
    Fractionally distil coal-tar light oil to draw off benzene; the heavy bottoms left in the column are coal-tar pitch.
    coaltar_distill_benzene
    DIdichlorohydrin×1 HAhydrochloric acid gas×1
    ACallyl chloride×1 CGchlorine gas×1 RWraw water×1 → DIdichlorohydrin×1 HAhydrochloric acid gas×1
    Oleum Absorption Tower 60s T5 +1 byproduct
    Chlorine dissolved in water makes hypochlorous acid, which adds across allyl chloride to give 1,3-dichloro-2-propanol (dichlorohydrin), releasing HCl.
    dichlorohydrin_hypochlorinate
    O(oleum (fuming sulfuric acid)×2
    STsulfur trioxide gas×3 RWraw water×1 → O(oleum (fuming sulfuric acid)×2
    Oleum Absorption Tower 20s T4 18 kJ
    Absorb SO3 into circulating strong acid: SO3 + H2SO4 -> H2S2O7 (oleum). SO3 is never fed to water directly -- the hydration is so violent it forms a fog of acid mist that will not condense. Make-up water keeps the loop charged.
    acid_form_oleum
    Pidgeon Retort 1
    MCmagnesium crown (crude)×2 DSdicalcium silicate slag×1
    CDcalcined dolime (MgO·CaO)×4 F(ferrosilicon (FeSi)×1 → MCmagnesium crown (crude)×2 DSdicalcium silicate slag×1
    Pidgeon Retort 300s T4 1470 kJ +1 byproduct
    The Pidgeon step: under vacuum at ~1200 C, silicon in ferrosilicon reduces magnesia (2 MgO.CaO + Si -> 2 Mg + Ca2SiO4). Magnesium boils off and condenses as a crown at the cold end; dicalcium-silicate slag stays behind.
    mg_pidgeon_reduce
    Powder Press and Sinter Furnace 6
    ACagglomerated cork×1
    COcork oak bark×2 → ACagglomerated cork×1
    Powder Press and Sinter Furnace 70s T6 90 kJ
    Granulate cork oak bark and bake it under pressure until its own resins bind it into board.
    re_cork_agglomerate
    UDuranium dioxide pellet×4
    EUenriched UO2 powder×2 → UDuranium dioxide pellet×4
    Powder Press and Sinter Furnace 120s T7 200 kJ
    Press the UO2 powder into pellets and sinter near 1700 C into dense, hard ceramic cylinders -- the actual fuel, fingertip-sized and good for years in-core.
    u_press_sinter_pellet
    SNsintered NdFeB magnet×2 SLslag×1
    NMneodymium metal×2 IIiron ingot×1 BOboric oxide (B2O3)×1 → SNsintered NdFeB magnet×2 SLslag×1
    Powder Press and Sinter Furnace 220s T6 240 kJ +1 byproduct
    Strip-cast and liquid-phase sinter neodymium, iron and boron into the Nd2Fe14B phase, then magnetise: a sintered NdFeB magnet, the strongest there is. Boron is small but non-negotiable -- it is what makes the phase exist.
    nd_make_ndfeb_magnet
    TCtantalum capacitor×2
    CGcapacitor-grade tantalum powder×3 → TCtantalum capacitor×2
    Powder Press and Sinter Furnace 80s T6 90 kJ
    Press and vacuum-sinter tantalum powder into a porous anode pellet, anodise an oxide dielectric onto it, and finish into a capacitor -- the most compact, reliable capacitor in electronics, and the reason anyone bothers separating tantalum from niobium.
    ta_make_capacitor
    CCcemented carbide insert×4
    TCtungsten carbide powder×4 CCcobalt cathode×1 → CCcemented carbide insert×4
    Powder Press and Sinter Furnace 150s T5 300 kJ
    Liquid-phase sinter tungsten-carbide powder with a COBALT binder (~6-10%) into a cemented-carbide insert: WC-Co, the genuine hardmetal grade used in real cutting tools. Cobalt wets and bonds WC grains far better than the old nickel stand-in.
    w_cemented_carbide
    STsintered tungsten rod×1
    TMtungsten metal powder×4 → STsintered tungsten rod×1
    Powder Press and Sinter Furnace 180s T5 360 kJ
    Press the powder into a bar and sinter it near 2500 C until the grains weld solid -- making a dense tungsten rod without ever melting the metal. This is powder metallurgy: the only way to consolidate the unmeltable.
    w_press_sinter_rod
    Precipitation Tank 11
    PAphosphoric acid×2 RGraw gypsum ore×3
    PRphosphate rock×3 SAsulfuric acid×3 → PAphosphoric acid×2 RGraw gypsum ore×3
    Precipitation Tank 80s T5 +1 byproduct
    Treat phosphate rock with sulfuric acid to make phosphoric acid, dropping gypsum as a byproduct (wet process). The gypsum here is the phosphogypsum the real process leaves behind.
    phosphoric_acid_wet_process
    AHammonium hexachloroiridate×1
    IRiridium residue×1 CGchlorine gas×2 ACammonium chloride×2 → AHammonium hexachloroiridate×1
    Precipitation Tank 130s T6 180 kJ
    Chlorinate the residue to soluble iridium chloride and precipitate it with ammonium chloride as ammonium hexachloroiridate.
    re_ammonium_hexachloroiridate
    BAbisphenol-A×1 RWraw water×1
    PHphenol×2 ACacetone×1 → BAbisphenol-A×1 RWraw water×1
    Precipitation Tank 80s T5 +1 byproduct
    Condense two phenols onto one acetone over an acid catalyst, splitting out water, to build bisphenol-A.
    bpa_condense
    EPepichlorohydrin×1 SLsalt lick×1 RWraw water×1
    DIdichlorohydrin×1 SHsodium hydroxide×1 → EPepichlorohydrin×1 SLsalt lick×1 RWraw water×1
    Precipitation Tank 70s T5 +2 byproducts
    Caustic soda strips HCl from dichlorohydrin and snaps the epoxide ring shut, giving epichlorohydrin; the lost chlorine leaves as common salt.
    epichlorohydrin_dehydrochlorinate
    ERepoxy resin×1 SLsalt lick×2 RWraw water×1
    BAbisphenol-A×1 EPepichlorohydrin×2 SHsodium hydroxide×2 → ERepoxy resin×1 SLsalt lick×2 RWraw water×1
    Precipitation Tank 110s T5 +2 byproducts
    Couple bisphenol-A with epichlorohydrin under caustic soda (the caustic-coupling process) to make liquid epoxy resin (DGEBA). The displaced chlorine leaves as salt.
    epoxy_resin_couple
    ANammonium nitrate×1
    NAnitric acid×1 AGammonia gas×1 → ANammonium nitrate×1
    Precipitation Tank 40s T5
    Neutralize nitric acid with ammonia to crystallize ammonium nitrate, a heavy nitrogen fertilizer.
    ammonium_nitrate_neutralize
    DPdiammonium phosphate×1
    PAphosphoric acid×1 AGammonia gas×2 → DPdiammonium phosphate×1
    Precipitation Tank 55s T5
    React phosphoric acid with ammonia to granulate diammonium phosphate, carrying both nitrogen and phosphorus.
    dap_ammoniate
    SSsingle superphosphate×2
    PRphosphate rock×2 SAsulfuric acid×1 → SSsingle superphosphate×2
    Precipitation Tank 60s T5
    Acidulate phosphate rock with sulfuric acid to make the phosphorus plant-available (single superphosphate).
    superphosphate_acidulate
    AHaluminum hydroxide (gibbsite)×2 SHsodium hydroxide×1
    SAsodium aluminate liquor×3 → AHaluminum hydroxide (gibbsite)×2 SHsodium hydroxide×1
    Precipitation Tank 180s T3 35 kJ +1 byproduct
    Cool the supersaturated liquor and seed it with fine gibbsite. Over hours, Al(OH)3 crystallises out: NaAlO2 + 2 H2O -> Al(OH)3 + NaOH. The freed caustic soda is pumped back to digestion -- the Bayer circuit recycles its own lye.
    al_precipitate_gibbsite
    IRiridium residue×1
    PCPGM concentrate×1 NAnitric acid×1 HAhydrochloric acid gas×3 → IRiridium residue×1
    Precipitation Tank 120s T6 170 kJ
    Digest the concentrate in aqua regia (nitric + hydrochloric); Pt, Pd and Au dissolve, leaving an insoluble Ir/Rh/Ru residue.
    re_iridium_residue
    PRPVC resin×2
    VCvinyl chloride monomer×4 → PRPVC resin×2
    Precipitation Tank 80s T5
    Link vinyl chloride units into PVC resin (suspension polymerization).
    pvc_polymerize
    Reactor Assembly Bay 1
    LWlight-water nuclear reactor×1
    EUenriched uranium fuel rod×4 HChafnium control rod×2 RSrolled steel plate×6 SCsteam control valve×2 LVlaboratory vacuum pump×1 TPthree-phase AC generator×1 → LWlight-water nuclear reactor×1
    Reactor Assembly Bay 400s T7 600 kJ
    Assemble fuel rods, hafnium control rods, a steel pressure vessel, steam valves, a vacuum/coolant pump and a three-phase generator into a light-water reactor: fission heats water, water drives the generator. The capstone of the nuclear branch.
    u_build_reactor
    Reagent Filling Line 4
    GCglass cell (water)×1
    GCglass cell×1 RWraw water×1 → GCglass cell (water)×1
    Reagent Filling Line 20s T3 15 kJ
    Charge a borosilicate cell with raw water and seal -- a clear, inert canister.
    cell_fill_glass_water
    MCmetal cell (sulfuric acid)×1
    MCmetal cell×1 SAsulfuric acid×1 → MCmetal cell (sulfuric acid)×1
    Reagent Filling Line 25s T4 20 kJ
    Charge a stainless cell with sulfuric acid. The stainless body earns its cost here -- acid would eat an ordinary can.
    cell_fill_metal_acid
    MCmetal cell (sodium hydroxide)×1
    MCmetal cell×1 SHsodium hydroxide×1 → MCmetal cell (sodium hydroxide)×1
    Reagent Filling Line 25s T4 20 kJ
    Charge a stainless cell with sodium hydroxide. Caustic in aluminium would dissolve the can and vent hydrogen; stainless holds it safely.
    cell_fill_metal_caustic
    MCmetal cell (water)×1
    MCmetal cell×1 RWraw water×1 → MCmetal cell (water)×1
    Reagent Filling Line 20s T4 15 kJ
    Charge a stainless cell with raw water and seal it -- the neutral reference fill.
    cell_fill_metal_water
    Reverberatory Furnace 2
    LClithium cobalt oxide cathode×1
    LClithium carbonate (battery grade)×1 RCroasted cobalt oxide×2 → LClithium cobalt oxide cathode×1
    Reverberatory Furnace
    Station: reverberatory_furnace
    itemdb_cathode_lithium_cobalt_oxide_reverberatory_furnace
    CMcopper matte×1 SLslag×2
    RCroasted chalcopyrite calcine×2 RSraw silica ore×1 CHcharcoal×2 → CMcopper matte×1 SLslag×2
    Reverberatory Furnace 240s T2 1850 kJ +1 byproduct
    Matte smelt with silica flux. Overall: 2CuFeS2 + 2SiO2 + 4O2 -> Cu2S + 2FeSiO3 + 3SO2. Iron leaves as iron-silicate (fayalite) slag; copper + sulfur collect as matte (Cu2S.FeS, ~45-60% Cu).
    cu_matte_smelt
    Roasting Pit 4
    RCroasted chalcopyrite calcine×2 SDsulfur dioxide gas×1
    CCchalcopyrite concentrate×3 → RCroasted chalcopyrite calcine×2 SDsulfur dioxide gas×1
    Roasting Pit 180s T1 505 kJ +1 byproduct
    Partial roast: 2CuFeS2 + O2 -> Cu2S + 2FeS + SO2. Burns off part of the sulfur (SO2 captured for the acid plant) and starts oxidizing iron. Mass drops with the lost sulfur.
    cu_roast_chalcopyrite
    RCroasted cobalt oxide×1 SDsulfur dioxide gas×1 ATarsenic trioxide (toxic)×1
    RCraw cobaltite ore×2 OGoxygen gas×1 → RCroasted cobalt oxide×1 SDsulfur dioxide gas×1 ATarsenic trioxide (toxic)×1
    Roasting Pit 100s T3 280 kJ +2 byproducts
    Dead-roast cobaltite (CoAsS): sulfur leaves as SO2 for the acid plant, arsenic is captured as toxic As2O3 from the flue rather than vented, and cobalt stays as a roasted oxide.
    co_roast_cobaltite
    NOnickel oxide sinter×1 SDsulfur dioxide gas×1
    NCnickel converter matte (Ni3S2)×1 OGoxygen gas×1 → NOnickel oxide sinter×1 SDsulfur dioxide gas×1
    Roasting Pit 90s T3 250 kJ +1 byproduct
    Dead-roast the converter matte to nickel oxide, driving off the last sulfur as SO2. Three SO2 streams from one metal -- sulfide nickel, like sulfide copper, always comes with an acid plant attached.
    nis_roast_matte
    ZOzinc oxide calcine×2 SDsulfur dioxide gas×1
    SCsphalerite concentrate×2 OGoxygen gas×1 → ZOzinc oxide calcine×2 SDsulfur dioxide gas×1
    Roasting Pit 110s T3 310 kJ +1 byproduct
    Roast the concentrate: ZnS burns to ZnO calcine, the sulfur leaving as SO2 for the contact acid plant. Both zinc routes start from this calcine.
    zn_roast
    Rotary Calciner Kiln 16
    BOboric oxide (B2O3)×2
    BOborax×2 → BOboric oxide (B2O3)×2
    Rotary Calciner Kiln 90s T4 695 kJ
    Calcine borax dry to boric oxide -- the boron the magnet phase needs.
    reb_calcine_boric_oxide
    S4Shell-405 catalyst×1
    ALalumina×1 ISiridium sponge×1 → S4Shell-405 catalyst×1
    Rotary Calciner Kiln 90s T6 150 kJ
    Impregnate an alumina support with iridium sponge and calcine into a Shell-405 hydrazine-decomposition catalyst bed.
    re_catalyst_shell405
    CDcalcined dolime (MgO·CaO)×2 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1
    DOdolomite×2 → CDcalcined dolime (MgO·CaO)×2 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1
    Rotary Calciner Kiln 90s T3 695 kJ +1 byproduct
    Calcine dolomite to dolime, driving off CO2 and leaving mixed MgO/CaO -- the charge the Pidgeon retort will reduce.
    mg_calcine_dolomite
    CPcalcined potters’ flint×2
    RFraw flint×2 → CPcalcined potters’ flint×2
    Rotary Calciner Kiln 200s T2 1540 kJ
    Calcine flint near 600 C until it crumbles, then grind to a fine silica powder (partly cristobalite). The classic potters’ flint for clay bodies and glazes.
    fl_calcine_silica
    SDsulfur dioxide gas×1 CCcement clinker×2
    RGraw gypsum ore×2 CFcoke fuel×1 RSraw silica ore×1 → SDsulfur dioxide gas×1 CCcement clinker×2
    Rotary Calciner Kiln 260s T4 2000 kJ +1 byproduct
    Mueller-Kuehne: starve gypsum of oxygen with coke at ~1400 C and it splits -- sulfur leaves as SO2 (sent to the contact acid plant) while the lime sinters with silica into cement clinker. One kiln makes both sulfuric acid AND cement, the classic move where native sulfur is scarce.
    gyp_muller_kuhne
    ZEzero-expansion glass-ceramic×1
    LAlithium-aluminosilicate melt×1 → ZEzero-expansion glass-ceramic×1
    Rotary Calciner Kiln 160s T6 180 kJ
    Hold the glass at its nucleation temperature so crystals grow through it, yielding a near-zero-expansion glass-ceramic.
    re_ceramize_zerodur
    ALalumina×2
    AHaluminum hydroxide (gibbsite)×3 → ALalumina×2
    Rotary Calciner Kiln 90s T3 695 kJ
    Fire the gibbsite white-hot in the rotary kiln to drive off its chemical water: 2 Al(OH)3 -> Al2O3 + 3 H2O. Out comes smelter-grade alumina, a free-flowing white sand.
    al_calcine_alumina
    RLreduced laterite calcine×3 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1
    NLnickel laterite (saprolite)×3 CFcoke fuel×1 → RLreduced laterite calcine×3 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1
    Rotary Calciner Kiln 90s T3 695 kJ +1 byproduct
    Tumble the wet saprolite down a fired rotary kiln to drive off free and crystal water and pre-reduce the NiO/FeO with coke and CO. The hot calcine is rushed straight to the furnace to keep its heat -- the R and K of RKEF.
    fni_calcine_laterite
    NPniobium pentoxide (Nb2O5)×1 HAhydrofluoric acid×1
    NFniobium fluoride raffinate×1 → NPniobium pentoxide (Nb2O5)×1 HAhydrofluoric acid×1
    Rotary Calciner Kiln 90s T5 695 kJ +1 byproduct
    Hydrolyse and calcine the niobium fluoride to white Nb2O5, driving off (and recovering) HF for re-use in digestion.
    nb_calcine_oxide
    POplaster of Paris×2
    RGraw gypsum ore×2 → POplaster of Paris×2
    Rotary Calciner Kiln 70s T2 540 kJ
    Gently calcine gypsum near 150 C to the hemihydrate -- plaster of Paris. Overshoot the temperature and you get dead-burnt anhydrite that will not set, so this is a low, controlled bake.
    gyp_calcine_plaster
    POplaster of Paris×2
    RGrefined gypsum×2 → POplaster of Paris×2
    Rotary Calciner Kiln 70s T3 540 kJ
    The HF chain throws off refined gypsum as a waste; calcine it the same way to plaster, turning a disposal problem into feedstock and closing the loop.
    gyp_recover_waste_plaster
    BSbeta-spodumene×2
    RSraw spodumene ore×2 → BSbeta-spodumene×2
    Rotary Calciner Kiln 180s T4 1385 kJ
    Decrepitate spodumene near 1100 C: the alpha crystal flips to the open beta form. Skip this and acid simply will not attack the ore -- the step everyone forgets.
    li_decrepitate
    SRsynthetic rutile×2 SLslag×1
    RIraw ilmenite ore×2 CFcoke fuel×1 → SRsynthetic rutile×2 SLslag×1
    Rotary Calciner Kiln 90s T3 695 kJ +1 byproduct
    Reductively roast ilmenite (FeTiO3) so the iron drops out as a metallic/oxide fraction, leaving high-grade synthetic rutile. Becher-style upgrading -- clean the feed before chlorination so you are not burning chlorine on iron.
    ti_upgrade_ilmenite
    TPtantalum pentoxide (Ta2O5)×1 HAhydrofluoric acid×1
    TFtantalum fluoride extract×1 → TPtantalum pentoxide (Ta2O5)×1 HAhydrofluoric acid×1
    Rotary Calciner Kiln 90s T5 695 kJ +1 byproduct
    Calcine the separated tantalum fluoride to Ta2O5, again recovering HF. The purified oxide is the gateway to capacitor-grade metal.
    ta_calcine_oxide
    TTtungsten trioxide×2 AGammonia gas×1 RWraw water×1
    APammonium paratungstate (APT)×2 → TTtungsten trioxide×2 AGammonia gas×1 RWraw water×1
    Rotary Calciner Kiln 90s T4 695 kJ +2 byproducts
    Calcine APT to canary-yellow tungsten trioxide: it decomposes to WO3 + NH3 + H2O, and the ammonia is captured and piped back to the crystalliser. Clean oxide out, reagent recovered.
    w_calcine_apt
    UDuranium dioxide (UO2)×3 RWraw water×2
    YEyellowcake×1 HGhydrogen gas×2 → UDuranium dioxide (UO2)×3 RWraw water×2
    Rotary Calciner Kiln 100s T6 770 kJ +1 byproduct
    Hydrogen-reduce yellowcake to uranium dioxide: U3O8 + 2 H2 -> 3 UO2 + 2 H2O. The first conversion step toward a fluoride.
    u_reduce_uo2
    Sheet Metal Press 2
    ASanti-slosh baffle×2
    ASaluminum sheet×1 → ASanti-slosh baffle×2
    Sheet Metal Press 40s T6 70 kJ
    Press aluminum ring baffles that damp propellant slosh inside the tank.
    re_baffle_antislosh
    MCmetal cell×2
    SSstainless steel sheet×2 → MCmetal cell×2
    Sheet Metal Press 60s T4 90 kJ
    Deep-draw and seam-weld stainless sheet into an empty cell body. Stainless, not plain steel or aluminium, because this one container has to survive both acid and caustic fills.
    cell_form_metal
    Siemens CVD Reactor 3
    HRhigh-reflector cavity mirror×1
    SPsuper-polished mirror flat×1 TPtantalum pentoxide (Ta2O5)×1 SFsynthetic fused silica×1 → HRhigh-reflector cavity mirror×1
    Siemens CVD Reactor 110s T6 170 kJ
    Sputter alternating quarter-wave tantala and fused-silica layers onto a polished flat to make a >99.99% dielectric high reflector.
    re_mirror_hr
    EGelectronic-grade polysilicon×1 HAhydrochloric acid gas×3 STsilicon tetrachloride×1
    TRtrichlorosilane×3 HGhydrogen gas×2 → EGelectronic-grade polysilicon×1 HAhydrochloric acid gas×3 STsilicon tetrachloride×1
    Siemens CVD Reactor 200s T4 380 kJ +2 byproducts
    The Siemens process: pass trichlorosilane and hydrogen over glowing silicon rods so hyper-pure silicon deposits atom by atom (SiHCl3 + H2 -> Si + 3 HCl). Recovers HCl for the chlorinator and sheds SiCl4 for redistribution. The single most energy-hungry step in all of electronics.
    si_siemens_polysilicon
    SFsynthetic fused silica×2
    STsilicon tetrachloride×1 OGoxygen gas×1 → SFsynthetic fused silica×2
    Siemens CVD Reactor 90s T6 150 kJ
    Flame-hydrolyse silicon tetrachloride into a pure SiO2 soot and fuse it into clear synthetic fused silica.
    re_fused_silica
    Sinter Strand 2
    LOlead oxide sinter×2 SDsulfur dioxide gas×1
    GCgalena concentrate×2 OGoxygen gas×1 → LOlead oxide sinter×2 SDsulfur dioxide gas×1
    Sinter Strand 110s T3 615 kJ +1 byproduct
    Roast-sinter the concentrate: PbS burns to PbO and a hard sinter cake, throwing SO2 to the contact acid plant -- sulfide metallurgy always feeds an acid plant.
    pb_sinter_roast
    IOiron ore sinter×3 SDsulfur dioxide gas×1
    IOiron ore concentrate×3 CFcoke fuel×1 LFlimestone flux×1 → IOiron ore sinter×3 SDsulfur dioxide gas×1
    Sinter Strand 90s T2 505 kJ +1 byproduct
    Fuse concentrate fines with coke breeze and limestone on a travelling-grate sinter strand into a hard, self-fluxing, permeable cake. Roasting drives sulfur off as SO2 -- pipe it to the acid plant instead of venting.
    iron_sinter
    Solvent Extraction Mixer-Settler 4
    PCpurified cobalt solution×1 NCnickel concentrate×1
    CScobalt sulfate liquor×2 → PCpurified cobalt solution×1 NCnickel concentrate×1
    Solvent Extraction Mixer-Settler 140s T4 120 kJ +1 byproduct
    Solvent-extract the liquor to separate cobalt from its near-twin nickel (and copper). Cobalt and nickel always travel together in nature, so the stripped nickel is recovered as concentrate, not wasted.
    co_solvent_extract
    RCrich copper electrolyte×1 CAchamber acid (dilute H2SO4)×1
    PLpregnant leach solution (PLS)×3 → RCrich copper electrolyte×1 CAchamber acid (dilute H2SO4)×1
    Solvent Extraction Mixer-Settler 40s T3 30 kJ +1 byproduct
    Mixer-settler solvent extraction: a LIX oxime dissolved in kerosene chelates Cu(2+) out of the PLS at pH<2.5 while rejecting iron, then is stripped by strong spent electrolyte into a clean, concentrated copper liquor. The organic recirculates indefinitely; the acid-rich, copper-stripped raffinate returns to the heap as the leach solution.
    cu_solvent_extract
    NOneodymium oxide×1 COcerium oxide×1 MRmixed REE raffinate×1
    MRmixed rare-earth sulfate×2 → NOneodymium oxide×1 COcerium oxide×1 MRmixed REE raffinate×1
    Solvent Extraction Mixer-Settler 260s T5 150 kJ +2 byproducts
    The keystone: a long counter-current solvent-extraction cascade exploits vanishingly small solubility differences to peel the lanthanides apart -- abundant cerium first, the prized neodymium next, the rest banked as raffinate. This single abstracted step stands in for hundreds of real mixer-settler stages.
    ree_sx_split
    NFniobium fluoride raffinate×1 TFtantalum fluoride extract×1
    CNcrude Nb/Ta fluoride liquor×2 → NFniobium fluoride raffinate×1 TFtantalum fluoride extract×1
    Solvent Extraction Mixer-Settler 160s T5 130 kJ +1 byproduct
    The keystone separation: run the mixed liquor through an MIBK solvent-extraction column to split niobium from tantalum -- chemical twins so alike that this is one of the hardest routine separations in metallurgy, and the reason tantalum is costly.
    nb_solvent_extract
    Steam Machine Shop 15
    CBCement Ball Mill×1
    RSrolled steel plate×8 SAsmall AC induction motor×1 TSturned steel shaft×1 RSrolled steel beam×4 SRsteel rivet×6 → CBCement Ball Mill×1
    Steam Machine Shop
    Station: machine shop (steam)
    itemdb_cement_ball_mill_machine_shop_steam
    CCCopper Converter×1
    RSrolled steel plate×6 SRsilica refractory brick×6 CTclay tuyere×4 SAsmall AC induction motor×1 → CCCopper Converter×1
    Steam Machine Shop
    Station: machine shop (steam)
    itemdb_copper_converter_machine_shop_steam
    BGbasic gas cylinder×2
    SIsteel ingot×1 → BGbasic gas cylinder×2
    Steam Machine Shop 70s T3 90 kJ
    Deep-draw and spin a steel billet into a seamless high-pressure cylinder, empty and ready to fill. The reusable container behind every compressed-gas product.
    gas_fab_cylinder
    FRFire Refining Furnace×1
    SRsilica refractory brick×6 RSrolled steel plate×4 CTclay tuyere×2 WIwrought-iron steam pipe×2 → FRFire Refining Furnace×1
    Steam Machine Shop
    Station: machine shop (steam)
    itemdb_fire_refining_furnace_machine_shop_steam
    FSFlash Smelting Furnace×1
    SRsilica refractory brick×8 RSrolled steel plate×6 CTclay tuyere×2 SRsteel rivet×4 → FSFlash Smelting Furnace×1
    Steam Machine Shop
    Station: machine shop (steam)
    itemdb_flash_smelting_furnace_machine_shop_steam
    GWGas Well Derrick×1
    RSrolled steel beam×8 RSrolled steel angle×4 SAsmall AC induction motor×1 SRsteel rivet×8 → GWGas Well Derrick×1
    Steam Machine Shop
    Station: machine shop (steam)
    itemdb_gas_well_derrick_machine_shop_steam
    LCLead Chamber×1
    RBrough board plank×6 LIlead ingot×4 BGborosilicate glass tube×2 FCfired clay brick×2 → LCLead Chamber×1
    Steam Machine Shop
    Station: machine shop (steam)
    itemdb_lead_chamber_machine_shop_steam
    LALi Acid-Roast Kiln×1
    RSrolled steel plate×8 SRsilica refractory brick×6 SAsmall AC induction motor×1 TSturned steel shaft×1 → LALi Acid-Roast Kiln×1
    Steam Machine Shop
    Station: machine shop (steam)
    itemdb_li_acid_roast_kiln_machine_shop_steam
    MFMannheim Furnace×1
    CIcast-iron firebox×4 SRsilica refractory brick×6 HIhammered iron plate×4 WIwrought-iron steam pipe×2 → MFMannheim Furnace×1
    Steam Machine Shop
    Station: machine shop (steam)
    itemdb_mannheim_furnace_machine_shop_steam
    PSpolyethylene sheet×4
    PRpolyethylene resin×2 → PSpolyethylene sheet×4
    Steam Machine Shop 40s T5
    Press polyethylene resin into flat sheet stock.
    pe_sheet_press
    PSpolypropylene sheet×4
    PRpolypropylene resin×2 → PSpolypropylene sheet×4
    Steam Machine Shop 40s T5
    Press polypropylene resin into flat sheet stock.
    pp_sheet_press
    PPPVC pipe×4
    PRPVC resin×2 → PPPVC pipe×4
    Steam Machine Shop 40s T5
    Extrude PVC resin into rigid pipe.
    pvc_pipe_extrude
    RCRotary Calciner Kiln×1
    RSrolled steel plate×8 SRsilica refractory brick×8 SAsmall AC induction motor×1 TSturned steel shaft×1 SRsteel rivet×4 → RCRotary Calciner Kiln×1
    Steam Machine Shop
    Station: machine shop (steam)
    itemdb_rotary_calciner_kiln_machine_shop_steam
    SSSinter Strand×1
    RSrolled steel plate×8 SRsteel rail×6 SAsmall AC induction motor×1 SRsteel rivet×4 → SSSinter Strand×1
    Steam Machine Shop
    Station: machine shop (steam)
    itemdb_sinter_strand_machine_shop_steam
    SBSulfur Burner×1
    HIhammered iron plate×4 FRfireclay refractory brick×4 WIwrought-iron steam pipe×2 HFhand-forged iron rivet×2 → SBSulfur Burner×1
    Steam Machine Shop
    Station: machine shop (steam)
    itemdb_sulfur_burner_machine_shop_steam
    Steam Reformer 3
    ETethylene×1 PRpropylene×1 MGmethane gas×1
    RNraw natural gas×3 SCsteam charge×1 → ETethylene×1 PRpropylene×1 MGmethane gas×1
    Steam Reformer 90s T5 630 kJ +2 byproducts
    Steam-crack the natural-gas liquids to light olefins -- ethylene plus a share of propylene -- leaving methane as the residual sales gas. Cracking is energy-heavy, so it burns a steam charge per cycle.
    ethylene_steam_crack
    HGhydrogen gas×4 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1
    SGsynthesis gas×3 RWraw water×1 → HGhydrogen gas×4 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1
    Steam Reformer 70s T4 490 kJ +1 byproduct
    Water-gas shift: CO + H2O -> CO2 + H2. Convert the carbon monoxide in syngas into still more hydrogen, leaving CO2 to vent or capture. This is the cheap bulk-hydrogen route most ammonia and refining actually run on.
    smr_water_gas_shift
    SGsynthesis gas×3
    MGmethane gas×1 RWraw water×2 → SGsynthesis gas×3
    Steam Reformer 90s T4 630 kJ
    Steam-methane reforming: CH4 + H2O -> CO + 3 H2 over a nickel catalyst at red heat. The workhorse first step of industrial hydrogen, turning methane and steam into synthesis gas.
    smr_steam_reform
    Steam Rolling Mill 6
    CFcopper foil×2
    CPcopper plate×1 → CFcopper foil×2
    Steam Rolling Mill 50s T3 55 kJ
    Cold-roll plate down to thin foil.
    cu_form_foil
    CFcopper foil×4
    CIcopper ingot×1 SCsteam charge×1 → CFcopper foil×4
    Steam Rolling Mill 30s T3
    The same job as hand-hammering, but the ingot is squeezed between powered rolls. It comes out as even sheet in seconds, and one ingot yields twice the foil because almost nothing is lost to scale and bad hammer blows. This is why the mill replaced the anvil for sheet work.
    mill_copper_foil_machined
    CPcopper plate×2
    CIcopper ingot×2 → CPcopper plate×2
    Steam Rolling Mill 60s T3 50 kJ
    Hot-roll copper ingot to plate stock.
    cu_form_plate
    CRcopper rod×2
    CIcopper ingot×1 → CRcopper rod×2
    Steam Rolling Mill 45s T3 40 kJ
    Roll/forge ingot to round rod stock.
    cu_form_rod
    SSstainless steel sheet×1
    SSstainless steel ingot×2 → SSstainless steel sheet×1
    Steam Rolling Mill
    chemName=StainlessSteel,formula=FeNiCr • Station: rolling_mill_steam
    itemdb_stainless_steel_sheet_rolling_mill_steam
    SRsteel rebar×3
    SIsteel ingot×1 → SRsteel rebar×3
    Steam Rolling Mill 60s T4 80 kJ
    Hot-roll steel into ribbed bar. The ribs grip concrete so steel and concrete pull together as one.
    bld_roll_rebar
    Stone Anvil 3
    CFcopper foil×2
    CIcopper ingot×1 → CFcopper foil×2
    Stone Anvil 50s T1
    Copper is soft enough to shape cold. Beat the ingot flat on the stone face, fold, and beat it down again until it spreads into thin sheet. The metal stiffens as you work it (it work-hardens), so smiths soften it in a fire between rounds (annealing). This cold-hammering on a stone anvil is the oldest metalworking there is.
    anvil_copper_foil_manual
    CRcopper rod×2
    CIcopper ingot×1 → CRcopper rod×2
    Stone Anvil 45s T1
    Hammer the ingot out long and roll it round against the stone into rod stock for fasteners, contacts, and small parts. Anneal in the fire when it starts to stiffen so it does not crack.
    anvil_copper_rod_manual
    HShafted steel knife×1
    SIsteel ingot×1 WHwood haft×1 ROrope×1 → HShafted steel knife×1
    Stone Anvil
    tier=4 • Station: anvil_stone
    itemdb_tool_steel_knife_hafted_anvil_stone
    Stone Mortar 4
    CGcrushed gravel×2
    FIfieldstone×1 → CGcrushed gravel×2
    Stone Mortar 40s T1 30 kJ
    Break stone into coarse, angular gravel for concrete, footings, and fill. Slow by hand, but stone is everywhere.
    bld_crush_gravel
    BPblack pigment×2
    CHcharcoal×1 → BPblack pigment×2
    Stone Mortar 35s T1 60 kJ
    Grind charcoal to a fine black carbon pigment.
    pig_grind_black
    ROred ochre pigment×2
    RHraw hematite ore×1 → ROred ochre pigment×2
    Stone Mortar 40s T1 70 kJ
    Grind hematite ore to a fine red ochre pigment.
    pig_grind_ochre
    WPwhite pigment×2
    BSburnt shell lime×1 → WPwhite pigment×2
    Stone Mortar 35s T1 60 kJ
    Grind burnt shell lime to a fine white powder pigment.
    pig_grind_white
    Submerged Arc Furnace 4
    FEferrochrome×2 SLslag×2 CDcarbon dioxide gas×2
    ICindurated chromite pellet×3 CFcoke fuel×3 BLburned lime (quicklime)×1 → FEferrochrome×2 SLslag×2 CDcarbon dioxide gas×2
    Submerged Arc Furnace 150s T4 380 kJ +2 byproducts
    Bury the electrodes in a charge of pellets, coke and lime and pour the power in: FeCr2O4 + 4 C -> Fe + 2 Cr + 4 CO. The carbon strips the oxygen, lime fluxes the Mg/Al/Si gangue into a fluid slag, and the CO off-gas burns off as CO2. Taps as high-carbon ferrochrome. One of the most energy-hungry steps in the whole tech tree.
    fcr_saf_smelt
    FEferronickel×2 SLslag×2
    RLreduced laterite calcine×3 CFcoke fuel×2 BLburned lime (quicklime)×1 → FEferronickel×2 SLslag×2
    Submerged Arc Furnace 150s T4 360 kJ +1 byproduct
    Reduction-smelt the hot calcine in the electric (submerged-arc) furnace. Nickel and a controlled amount of iron drop out together as a dense crude ferronickel; the silicate gangue floats off as slag. ~99% of the nickel is recovered -- the E and F of RKEF.
    fni_smelt_ferronickel
    F(ferrosilicon (FeSi)×1 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1
    RSraw silica ore×2 PIpig iron ingot×1 CFcoke fuel×2 → F(ferrosilicon (FeSi)×1 CDcarbon dioxide gas×1
    Submerged Arc Furnace 130s T3 260 kJ +1 byproduct
    Smelt silica with iron and coke in a submerged-arc furnace to ferrosilicon -- a steel deoxidiser, and here the reductant that will free magnesium from dolime.
    mg_make_ferrosilicon
    MGmetallurgical-grade silicon×1 CDcarbon dioxide gas×2
    RSraw silica ore×2 CFcoke fuel×2 → MGmetallurgical-grade silicon×1 CDcarbon dioxide gas×2
    Submerged Arc Furnace 120s T3 260 kJ +1 byproduct
    Reduce quartz with carbon in a submerged-arc furnace at ~1900C: SiO2 + 2 C -> Si + 2 CO. Brutally energy-hungry (~11 kWh/kg), and the product is still only ~98% silicon -- metallurgical grade, good for alloys but far too dirty for chips.
    si_carbothermic_silicon
    Sulfur Burner / Pyrite Roaster 2
    SDsulfur dioxide gas×2
    RSrecovered sulfur×2 OGoxygen gas×2 → SDsulfur dioxide gas×2
    Sulfur Burner / Pyrite Roaster 40s T4 85 kJ
    Burn recovered sulfur in air: S + O2 -> SO2. A cleaner SO2 feed for the contact sulfuric-acid plant than roasting pyrite -- and, in the real world, the dominant modern route to acid.
    ng_burn_sulfur
    SDsulfur dioxide gas×6 RHroasted hematite×2
    RPraw pyrite ore×4 → SDsulfur dioxide gas×6 RHroasted hematite×2
    Sulfur Burner / Pyrite Roaster 50s T2 105 kJ +1 byproduct
    Dead-roast pyrite in excess air: 4 FeS2 + 11 O2 -> 2 Fe2O3 + 8 SO2. Drives off the SO2 acid feed AND leaves an iron-oxide cinder -- the same roasted hematite the iron chain smelts. One of the oldest acid feedstocks; the other is captured copper-smelter SO2.
    acid_roast_pyrite
    TiCl4 Distillation Column 1
    TTtitanium tetrachloride×2 TAtailings×1
    CTcrude titanium tetrachloride×2 → TTtitanium tetrachloride×2 TAtailings×1
    TiCl4 Distillation Column 80s T4 90 kJ +1 byproduct
    Fractionally distil the crude tickle to strip iron, vanadium and silicon chlorides. Purity here decides the grade of metal at the far end -- aerospace titanium starts with a clean distillation.
    ti_distill_ticl4
    Trip Hammer 10
    CDcopper dust×2
    CIcopper ingot×1 → CDcopper dust×2
    Trip Hammer 30s T1
    File/grind ingot to copper powder for alloying and powder metallurgy.
    cu_form_dust
    CBcrushed bauxite×2 SDstone dust×1
    RBraw bauxite ore×2 → CBcrushed bauxite×2 SDstone dust×1
    Trip Hammer 30s T2 16 kJ +1 byproduct
    Mill the bauxite fine so hot caustic can reach every grain. No flotation here -- bauxite is refined chemically, not concentrated mechanically.
    al_crush_bauxite
    CCcrushed chalcopyrite×5 SDstone dust×1
    RCraw chalcopyrite ore×6 → CCcrushed chalcopyrite×5 SDstone dust×1
    Trip Hammer 45s T1 +1 byproduct
    Coarse stamp-crush to liberate chalcopyrite from gangue. ~1 in 6 reports to fines as stone dust. Muscle/trip-hammer -- pay in time, not energy.
    cu_crush_chalcopyrite
    CCcrushed chromite ore×2 SDstone dust×1
    RCraw chromite ore×2 → CCcrushed chromite ore×2 SDstone dust×1
    Trip Hammer 30s T2 16 kJ +1 byproduct
    Crush friable lump chromite to an even grain. Chromite is brittle and sheds fines, so it is crushed deliberately and then re-agglomerated rather than charged as-mined.
    fcr_crush_chromite
    COcrushed oxide copper ore×2 SDstone dust×1
    OCoxidized copper ore×2 → COcrushed oxide copper ore×2 SDstone dust×1
    Trip Hammer 25s T1 12 kJ +1 byproduct
    Crush oxidized ore to a coarse, permeable gravel (12-50 mm). Oxide heaps want a COARSE crush -- fine grinding would only choke acid percolation and burn extra acid on freshly exposed gangue.
    cu_crush_oxide
    GCground chalcopyrite×4 SDstone dust×1
    CCcrushed chalcopyrite×5 → GCground chalcopyrite×4 SDstone dust×1
    Trip Hammer 60s T1 +1 byproduct
    Fine grind to ~75 micron flotation size. Over-grind sheds a little more stone dust.
    cu_grind_chalcopyrite
    IOiron ore concentrate×1
    IOiron ore rock×1 → IOiron ore concentrate×1
    Trip Hammer
    Station: stamp_mill_manual_trip_hammer • Byproduct: tailings
    itemdb_ore_iron_concentrate_stamp_mill_manual_trip_hammer
    PPpaper pulp×1
    PFplant fibers×2 RWraw water×1 WAwood ash×1 → PPpaper pulp×1
    Trip Hammer 70s T1 180 kJ
    Soak plant fibre in water with a little wood-ash lye and beat it under the trip hammer into a pulp.
    ppr_pulp_fiber
    PPpaper pulp×2
    LClinen cloth×1 RWraw water×1 → PPpaper pulp×2
    Trip Hammer 60s T1 180 kJ
    Beat linen rag in water under the trip hammer into a fine, high-yield rag pulp.
    ppr_pulp_rag
    PPpaper pulp×2
    SLstripped log×1 RWraw water×1 SLslaked lime×1 → PPpaper pulp×2
    Trip Hammer 110s T1 200 kJ
    Cook wood with slaked lime and beat it into a coarse wood pulp at the trip hammer.
    ppr_pulp_wood
    Uranium Conversion Reactor 3
    UHuranium hexafluoride×1
    UTuranium tetrafluoride (UF4)×1 FGfluorine gas×1 → UHuranium hexafluoride×1
    Uranium Conversion Reactor 90s T6 150 kJ
    Fluorinate the green salt with elemental fluorine: UF4 + F2 -> UF6. The product sublimes to a gas at gentle heat -- the one uranium compound you can pump through a centrifuge.
    u_fluorinate_uf6
    EUenriched UO2 powder×1 HAhydrofluoric acid×2
    EUenriched UF6 (LEU)×1 RWraw water×2 HGhydrogen gas×1 → EUenriched UO2 powder×1 HAhydrofluoric acid×2
    Uranium Conversion Reactor 110s T7 160 kJ +1 byproduct
    Hydrolyse and hydrogen-reduce enriched UF6 back to UO2 powder, recovering the fluorine as HF (fed straight back to conversion). Closing the fluorine loop is what makes conversion economic.
    u_reconvert_uo2_powder
    UTuranium tetrafluoride (UF4)×1 RWraw water×2
    UDuranium dioxide (UO2)×1 HAhydrofluoric acid×4 → UTuranium tetrafluoride (UF4)×1 RWraw water×2
    Uranium Conversion Reactor 100s T6 140 kJ +1 byproduct
    React UO2 with hydrogen fluoride: UO2 + 4 HF -> UF4 + 2 H2O. The oxide becomes the green salt, halfway to the volatile hexafluoride.
    u_hydrofluorinate_uf4
    Vacuum Arc Remelt Furnace 3
    TItitanium ingot×1
    TStitanium sponge×3 → TItitanium ingot×1
    Vacuum Arc Remelt Furnace 180s T4 1385 kJ
    Crush and compact the sponge into an electrode, then vacuum-arc-remelt it into a dense, homogeneous ingot -- often twice for aerospace grades. The vacuum keeps reactive titanium away from the oxygen and nitrogen it loves.
    ti_var_ingot
    ITInconel turbine rotor×1
    ISInconel superalloy ingot×2 → ITInconel turbine rotor×1
    Vacuum Arc Remelt Furnace 90s T6 220 kJ
    Cast and finish a bladed Inconel turbine wheel that survives the gas-generator exhaust.
    re_turbine_rotor
    ZIzirconium ingot×1
    ZSzirconium sponge×3 → ZIzirconium ingot×1
    Vacuum Arc Remelt Furnace 180s T4 1385 kJ
    Crush and compact the sponge into an electrode, then vacuum-arc-remelt it into a dense, homogeneous ingot. The vacuum keeps reactive zirconium clear of the oxygen and nitrogen it would otherwise gulp down.
    zr_var_ingot
    Wafer Fab Line 2
    PSpolished silicon wafer×1
    RSraw silicon wafer×1 → PSpolished silicon wafer×1
    Wafer Fab Line 50s T4 70 kJ
    Chemically-mechanically polish (CMP) an as-cut wafer to an atomically smooth, defect-free mirror -- the pristine substrate fine logic circuits demand.
    si_polish_wafer
    RSraw silicon wafer×8
    CSCzochralski silicon ingot×1 → RSraw silicon wafer×8
    Wafer Fab Line 60s T4 80 kJ
    Slice the monocrystal ingot into thin wafers with a wire saw and lap them flat. As-cut wafers are matte and saw-damaged -- ready for rugged devices or for polishing.
    si_slice_wafer
    Workbench 24
    SBsteel beam×1
    RSrolled steel beam×1 SRsteel rivet×4 → SBsteel beam×1
    Workbench 70s T4
    Rivet up a steel span to carry heavy loads across wide openings no timber could manage.
    bld_build_beam_steel
    BBblank book×1
    PApaper×5 LTlight tanned leather×1 PTplant thread×2 AGanimal glue×1 → BBblank book×1
    Workbench 90s T1 70 kJ
    Fold and stitch paper sheets into a block, glue the spine, and bind it into leather covers to make a blank book.
    book_bind_blank
    LClinen cloth×1
    PTplant thread×3 → LClinen cloth×1
    Workbench 120s T1 200 kJ
    Weave spun plant thread into a bolt of light, breathable linen-type cloth.
    tex_weave_linen_cloth
    WCwool cloth×1
    WYwool yarn×3 → WCwool cloth×1
    Workbench 120s T1 200 kJ
    Warp the loom and weave wool yarn into a bolt of warm, hard-wearing cloth.
    tex_weave_wool_cloth
    COcopperas×2
    IIiron ingot×1 CAchamber acid (dilute H2SO4)×1 → COcopperas×2
    Workbench 80s T3 110 kJ
    Dissolve iron in dilute sulfuric acid and evaporate to pale green copperas crystals (iron sulfate).
    ink_make_copperas
    WFwool felt×1
    WWwashed wool×2 RWraw water×1 → WFwool felt×1
    Workbench 90s T1 200 kJ
    Agitate washed wool in hot water until the fibres lock together into a dense felt -- no spinning needed.
    tex_felt_wool
    FAflint-and-steel striker×2
    RFraw flint×1 SIsteel ingot×1 → FAflint-and-steel striker×2
    Workbench 60s T2 25 kJ
    Forge/fit a high-carbon steel striker to a flint nodule. Real flint-and-steel: the harder flint shaves heated steel particles that ignite -- the most primitive ore meets the steel chain.
    fl_fire_steel
    CSconcrete slab×1
    WCwet concrete×4 SRsteel rebar×2 → CSconcrete slab×1
    Workbench 120s T4
    Cast a rebar slab to span wider and carry far more than timber. Heavy, so it needs solid supports beneath.
    bld_build_floor_concrete
    CFconcrete footing×1
    WCwet concrete×4 SRsteel rebar×2 → CFconcrete footing×1
    Workbench 120s T4
    Pour a rebar-tied concrete footing, the strongest anchor and the best base for tall or heavy builds.
    bld_build_foundation_concrete
    SFstone footing×1
    COcobblestone×6 LMlime mortar×2 → SFstone footing×1
    Workbench 90s T2
    Bed heavy stone in mortar to spread the load into the ground: a strong, low anchor for everything above.
    bld_build_foundation_stone
    HLHeap Leach Pad×1
    PPPVC pipe×4 RRrounded river stone×4 RBrough board plank×2 → HLHeap Leach Pad×1
    Workbench
    Station: workbench
    itemdb_heap_leach_pad_workbench
    CIcarbon ink×2
    BPblack pigment×1 AGanimal glue×1 RWraw water×1 → CIcarbon ink×2
    Workbench 45s T1 60 kJ
    Suspend black carbon pigment in glue and water to make a writing ink.
    ink_make_carbon
    IGiron gall ink×2
    CBcurled bark strip×2 COcopperas×1 RWraw water×1 → IGiron gall ink×2
    Workbench 70s T1 70 kJ
    Steep bark tannin in water and react it with copperas to make dark, permanent iron-gall ink.
    ink_make_iron_gall
    KSKnapping Station×1
    RRrounded river stone×2 RTrough timber beam×1 RCrawhide cord×1 → KSKnapping Station×1
    Workbench
    Station: workbench
    itemdb_knapping_station_workbench
    LYlye×1
    WAwood ash×2 RWraw water×1 → LYlye×1
    Workbench 50s T1 40 kJ
    Trickle water through wood ash to leach out a caustic lye solution.
    soap_leach_lye
    BPbrick pier×1
    FCfired clay brick×8 CMcement mortar×2 → BPbrick pier×1
    Workbench 90s T3
    Lay fired brick in mortar into a stout pier: heavy, fireproof, and stiff against both load and sway.
    bld_build_pillar_brick
    CCconcrete column×1
    RCreinforced concrete×3 → CCconcrete column×1
    Workbench 90s T4
    Cast a rebar-cored column, the workhorse vertical support for tall builds.
    bld_build_pillar_concrete
    TRtiled roof×1
    RTrough timber beam×2 RBrough board plank×2 CRclay roof tile×10 → TRtiled roof×1
    Workbench 100s T2
    Lap fired clay tiles over a timber frame. Heavy and slower to build, but fireproof and long-lasting.
    bld_build_roof_tiled
    SSstone stair×1
    COcobblestone×8 LMlime mortar×2 → SSstone stair×1
    Workbench 90s T2
    Set stone steps in mortar: heavy and slow, but fireproof and effectively permanent.
    bld_build_stair_stone
    PTplant thread×1
    PFplant fibers×2 → PTplant thread×1
    Workbench 60s T1 170 kJ
    Spin retted plant fibres into a fine, strong thread.
    tex_spin_plant_thread
    BWbrick wall×1
    FCfired clay brick×10 CMcement mortar×3 → BWbrick wall×1
    Workbench 90s T3
    Lay fired brick in mortar into a load-bearing wall that can carry a floor or roof directly.
    bld_build_wall_brick
    CWconcrete wall×1
    WCwet concrete×4 SRsteel rebar×1 → CWconcrete wall×1
    Workbench 90s T4
    Pour a concrete wall around light rebar: a dense, weatherproof shell that doubles as structure.
    bld_build_wall_concrete
    WRwool roving×1
    WWwashed wool×1 → WRwool roving×1
    Workbench 45s T1 180 kJ
    Comb washed wool so the fibres run parallel, drawing it into a soft roving sliver.
    tex_card_wool
    WYwool yarn×1
    WRwool roving×1 → WYwool yarn×1
    Workbench 75s T1 180 kJ
    Draw and twist the roving into a strong continuous wool yarn on the spindle.
    tex_spin_wool_yarn
    Yellowcake Precipitator 1
    YEyellowcake×1 TAtailings×1
    UPuranium pregnant liquor×3 AGammonia gas×1 → YEyellowcake×1 TAtailings×1
    Yellowcake Precipitator 120s T6 110 kJ +1 byproduct
    Concentrate the liquor (solvent extraction / ion exchange) and precipitate ammonium diuranate with ammonia, then calcine it to bright-yellow U3O8 -- yellowcake, the form uranium is actually shipped in.
    u_precipitate_yellowcake

    Conduvia — a field manual for rebuilding the world.
